file.content:
<task id="_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml" name="Execute Workflow" internal="true">
  <objective>Execute given workflow by loading its configuration, following instructions, and producing output</objective>

  <llm critical="true">
    <mandate>Always read COMPLETE files - NEVER use offset/limit when reading any workflow related files</mandate>
    <mandate>Instructions are MANDATORY - either as file path, steps or embedded list in YAML, XML or markdown</mandate>
    <mandate>Execute ALL steps in instructions IN EXACT ORDER</mandate>
    <mandate>Save to template output file after EVERY "template-output" tag</mandate>
    <mandate>NEVER skip a step - YOU are responsible for every steps execution without fail or excuse</mandate>
  </llm>

  <WORKFLOW-RULES critical="true">
    <rule n="1">Steps execute in exact numerical order (1, 2, 3...)</rule>
    <rule n="2">Optional steps: Ask user unless #yolo mode active</rule>
    <rule n="3">Template-output tags: Save content, discuss with the user the section completed, and NEVER proceed until the users indicates
      to proceed (unless YOLO mode has been activated)</rule>
  </WORKFLOW-RULES>

  <flow>
    <step n="1" title="Load and Initialize Workflow">
      <substep n="1a" title="Load Configuration and Resolve Variables">
        <action>Read workflow.yaml from provided path</action>
        <mandate>Load config_source (REQUIRED for all modules)</mandate>
        <phase n="1">Load external config from config_source path</phase>
        <phase n="2">Resolve all {config_source}: references with values from config</phase>
        <phase n="3">Resolve system variables (date:system-generated) and paths ({project-root}, {installed_path})</phase>
        <phase n="4">Ask user for input of any variables that are still unknown</phase>
      </substep>

      <substep n="1b" title="Load Required Components">
        <mandate>Instructions: Read COMPLETE file from path OR embedded list (REQUIRED)</mandate>
        <check>If template path → Read COMPLETE template file</check>
        <check>If validation path → Note path for later loading when needed</check>
        <check>If template: false → Mark as action-workflow (else template-workflow)</check>
        <note>Data files (csv, json) → Store paths only, load on-demand when instructions reference them</note>
      </substep>

      <substep n="1c" title="Initialize Output" if="template-workflow">
        <action>Resolve default_output_file path with all variables and {{date}}</action>
        <action>Create output directory if doesn't exist</action>
        <action>If template-workflow → Write template to output file with placeholders</action>
        <action>If action-workflow → Skip file creation</action>
      </substep>
    </step>

    <step n="2" title="Process Each Instruction Step in Order">
      <iterate>For each step in instructions:</iterate>

      <substep n="2a" title="Handle Step Attributes">
        <check>If optional="true" and NOT #yolo → Ask user to include</check>
        <check>If if="condition" → Evaluate condition</check>
        <check>If for-each="item" → Repeat step for each item</check>
        <check>If repeat="n" → Repeat step n times</check>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2b" title="Execute Step Content">
        <action>Process step instructions (markdown or XML tags)</action>
        <action>Replace {{variables}} with values (ask user if unknown)</action>
        <execute-tags>
          <tag>action xml tag → Perform the action</tag>
          <tag>check if="condition" xml tag → Conditional block wrapping actions (requires closing </check>)</tag>
          <tag>ask xml tag → Prompt user and WAIT for response</tag>
          <tag>invoke-workflow xml tag → Execute another workflow with given inputs and the workflow.xml runner</tag>
          <tag>invoke-task xml tag → Execute specified task</tag>
          <tag>invoke-protocol name="protocol_name" xml tag → Execute reusable protocol from protocols section</tag>
          <tag>goto step="x" → Jump to specified step</tag>
        </execute-tags>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2c" title="Handle template-output Tags">
        <if tag="template-output">
          <mandate>Generate content for this section</mandate>
          <mandate>Save to file (Write first time, Edit subsequent)</mandate>
          <action>Display generated content</action>
          <ask> [a] Advanced Elicitation, [c] Continue, [p] Party-Mode, [y] YOLO the rest of this document only. WAIT for response. <if
              response="a">
              <action>Start the advanced elicitation workflow {project-root}/_bmad/core/workflows/advanced-elicitation/workflow.xml</action>
            </if>
            <if
              response="c">
              <action>Continue to next step</action>
            </if>
            <if response="p">
              <action>Start the party-mode workflow {project-root}/_bmad/core/workflows/party-mode/workflow.md</action>
            </if>
            <if
              response="y">
              <action>Enter #yolo mode for the rest of the workflow</action>
            </if>
          </ask>
        </if>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2d" title="Step Completion">
        <check>If no special tags and NOT #yolo:</check>
        <ask>Continue to next step? (y/n/edit)</ask>
      </substep>
    </step>

    <step n="3" title="Completion">
      <check>Confirm document saved to output path</check>
      <action>Report workflow completion</action>
    </step>
  </flow>

  <execution-modes>
    <mode name="normal">Full user interaction and confirmation of EVERY step at EVERY template output - NO EXCEPTIONS except yolo MODE</mode>
    <mode name="yolo">Skip all confirmations and elicitation, minimize prompts and try to produce all of the workflow automatically by
      simulating the remaining discussions with an simulated expert user</mode>
  </execution-modes>

  <supported-tags desc="Instructions can use these tags">
    <structural>
      <tag>step n="X" goal="..." - Define step with number and goal</tag>
      <tag>optional="true" - Step can be skipped</tag>
      <tag>if="condition" - Conditional execution</tag>
      <tag>for-each="collection" - Iterate over items</tag>
      <tag>repeat="n" - Repeat n times</tag>
    </structural>
    <execution>
      <tag>action - Required action to perform</tag>
      <tag>action if="condition" - Single conditional action (inline, no closing tag needed)</tag>
      <tag>check if="condition">...</check> - Conditional block wrapping multiple items (closing tag required)</tag>
      <tag>ask - Get user input (ALWAYS wait for response before continuing)</tag>
      <tag>goto - Jump to another step</tag>
      <tag>invoke-workflow - Call another workflow</tag>
      <tag>invoke-task - Call a task</tag>
      <tag>invoke-protocol - Execute a reusable protocol (e.g., discover_inputs)</tag>
    </execution>
    <output>
      <tag>template-output - Save content checkpoint</tag>
      <tag>critical - Cannot be skipped</tag>
      <tag>example - Show example output</tag>
    </output>
  </supported-tags>

  <protocols desc="Reusable workflow protocols that can be invoked via invoke-protocol tag">
    <protocol name="discover_inputs" desc="Smart file discovery and loading based on input_file_patterns">
      <objective>Intelligently load project files (whole or sharded) based on workflow's input_file_patterns configuration</objective>

      <critical>Only execute if workflow.yaml contains input_file_patterns section</critical>

      <flow>
        <step n="1" title="Parse Input File Patterns">
          <action>Read input_file_patterns from loaded workflow.yaml</action>
          <action>For each pattern group (prd, architecture, epics, etc.), note the load_strategy if present</action>
        </step>

        <step n="2" title="Load Files Using Smart Strategies">
          <iterate>For each pattern in input_file_patterns:</iterate>

          <substep n="2a" title="Try Sharded Documents First">
            <check if="sharded pattern exists">
              <action>Determine load_strategy from pattern config (defaults to FULL_LOAD if not specified)</action>

              <strategy name="FULL_LOAD">
                <desc>Load ALL files in sharded directory - used for PRD, Architecture, UX, brownfield docs</desc>
                <action>Use glob pattern to find ALL .md files (e.g., "{output_folder}/*architecture*/*.md")</action>
                <action>Load EVERY matching file completely</action>
                <action>Concatenate content in logical order (index.md first if exists, then alphabetical)</action>
                <action>Store in variable: {pattern_name_content}</action>
              </strategy>

              <strategy name="SELECTIVE_LOAD">
                <desc>Load specific shard using template variable - example: used for epics with {{epic_num}}</desc>
                <action>Check for template variables in sharded_single pattern (e.g., {{epic_num}})</action>
                <action>If variable undefined, ask user for value OR infer from context</action>
                <action>Resolve template to specific file path</action>
                <action>Load that specific file</action>
                <action>Store in variable: {pattern_name_content}</action>
              </strategy>

              <strategy name="INDEX_GUIDED">
                <desc>Load index.md, analyze structure and description of each doc in the index, then intelligently load relevant docs</desc>
                <mandate>DO NOT BE LAZY - use best judgment to load documents that might have relevant information, even if only a 5% chance</mandate>
                <action>Load index.md from sharded directory</action>
                <action>Parse table of contents, links, section headers</action>
                <action>Analyze workflow's purpose and objective</action>
                <action>Identify which linked/referenced documents are likely relevant</action>
                <example>If workflow is about authentication and index shows "Auth Overview", "Payment Setup", "Deployment" → Load auth
                  docs, consider deployment docs, skip payment</example>
                <action>Load all identified relevant documents</action>
                <action>Store combined content in variable: {pattern_name_content}</action>
                <note>When in doubt, LOAD IT - context is valuable, being thorough is better than missing critical info</note>
              </strategy>
              <action>Mark pattern as RESOLVED, skip to next pattern</action>
            </check>
          </substep>

          <substep n="2b" title="Try Whole Document if No Sharded Found">
            <check if="no sharded matches found OR no sharded pattern exists">
              <action>Attempt glob match on 'whole' pattern (e.g., "{output_folder}/*prd*.md")</action>
              <check if="matches found">
                <action>Load ALL matching files completely (no offset/limit)</action>
                <action>Store content in variable: {pattern_name_content} (e.g., {prd_content})</action>
                <action>Mark pattern as RESOLVED, skip to next pattern</action>
              </check>
            </check>
          </substep>

          <substep n="2c" title="Handle Not Found">
            <check if="no matches for sharded OR whole">
              <action>Set {pattern_name_content} to empty string</action>
              <action>Note in session: "No {pattern_name} files found" (not an error, just unavailable, offer use change to provide)</action>
            </check>
          </substep>
        </step>

        <step n="3" title="Report Discovery Results">
          <action>List all loaded content variables with file counts</action>
          <example>
            ✓ Loaded {prd_content} from 5 sharded files: prd/index.md, prd/requirements.md, ...
            ✓ Loaded {architecture_content} from 1 file: Architecture.md
            ✓ Loaded {epics_content} from selective load: epics/epic-3.md
            ○ No ux_design files found
          </example>
          <note>This gives workflow transparency into what context is available</note>
        </step>
      </flow>

    </protocol>
  </protocols>

  <llm final="true">
    <critical-rules>
      • This is the complete workflow execution engine
      • You MUST Follow instructions exactly as written
      • The workflow execution engine is governed by: {project-root}/_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml
      • You MUST have already loaded and processed: {installed_path}/workflow.yaml
      • This workflow uses INTENT-DRIVEN PLANNING - adapt organically to product type and context
      • YOU ARE FACILITATING A CONVERSATION With a user to produce a final document step by step. The whole process is meant to be
      collaborative helping the user flesh out their ideas. Do not rush or optimize and skip any section.
    </critical-rules>
  </llm>
</task>

file.content:
# Review Story Workflow
name: code-review
description: 'Perform adversarial code review finding specific issues. Use when the user says "run code review" or "review this code"'

# Critical variables from config
config_source: "{project-root}/_bmad/bmm/config.yaml"
user_name: "{config_source}:user_name"
communication_language: "{config_source}:communication_language"
user_skill_level: "{config_source}:user_skill_level"
document_output_language: "{config_source}:document_output_language"
date: system-generated
planning_artifacts: "{config_source}:planning_artifacts"
implementation_artifacts: "{config_source}:implementation_artifacts"
sprint_status: "{implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml"

# Workflow components
installed_path: "{project-root}/_bmad/bmm/workflows/4-implementation/code-review"
instructions: "{installed_path}/instructions.xml"
validation: "{installed_path}/checklist.md"
template: false

project_context: "**/project-context.md"

# Smart input file references - handles both whole docs and sharded docs
# Priority: Whole document first, then sharded version
# Strategy: SELECTIVE LOAD - only load the specific epic needed for this story review
input_file_patterns:
  architecture:
    description: "System architecture for review context"
    whole: "{planning_artifacts}/*architecture*.md"
    sharded: "{planning_artifacts}/*architecture*/*.md"
    load_strategy: "FULL_LOAD"
  ux_design:
    description: "UX design specification (if UI review)"
    whole: "{planning_artifacts}/*ux*.md"
    sharded: "{planning_artifacts}/*ux*/*.md"
    load_strategy: "FULL_LOAD"
  epics:
    description: "Epic containing story being reviewed"
    whole: "{planning_artifacts}/*epic*.md"
    sharded_index: "{planning_artifacts}/*epic*/index.md"
    sharded_single: "{planning_artifacts}/*epic*/epic-{{epic_num}}.md"
    load_strategy: "SELECTIVE_LOAD"

  <step n="1" goal="Load story and discover changes">

    </action>

    <!-- Code Quality Deep Dive -->
    <action>For EACH file in comprehensive review list:
      1. **Security**: Look for injection risks, missing validation, auth issues
      2. **Performance**: N+1 queries, inefficient loops, missing caching
      3. **Error Handling**: Missing try/catch, poor error messages
      4. **Code Quality**: Complex functions, magic numbers, poor naming
      5. **Test Quality**: Are tests real assertions or placeholders?
    </action>

    <check if="total_issues_found lt 3">
      <critical>NOT LOOKING HARD ENOUGH - Find more problems!</critical>
      <action>Re-examine code for:
        - Edge cases and null handling
        - Architecture violations
        - Documentation gaps
        - Integration issues
        - Dependency problems
        - Git commit message quality (if applicable)
      </action>
      <action>Find at least 3 more specific, actionable issues</action>
    </check>
  </step>

  <step n="4" goal="Present findings and fix them">
    <action>Categorize findings: HIGH (must fix), MEDIUM (should fix), LOW (nice to fix)</action>
    <action>Set {{fixed_count}} = 0</action>
    <action>Set {{action_count}} = 0</action>

    <output>**🔥 CODE REVIEW FINDINGS, {user_name}!**

      **Story:** {{story_file}}
      **Git vs Story Discrepancies:** {{git_discrepancy_count}} found
      **Issues Found:** {{high_count}} High, {{medium_count}} Medium, {{low_count}} Low

      ## 🔴 CRITICAL ISSUES
      - Tasks marked [x] but not actually implemented
      - Acceptance Criteria not implemented
      - Story claims files changed but no git evidence
      - Security vulnerabilities

      ## 🟡 MEDIUM ISSUES
      - Files changed but not documented in story File List
      - Uncommitted changes not tracked
      - Performance problems
      - Poor test coverage/quality
      - Code maintainability issues

      ## 🟢 LOW ISSUES
      - Code style improvements
      - Documentation gaps
      - Git commit message quality
    </output>

    <ask>What should I do with these issues?

      1. **Fix them automatically** - I'll update the code and tests
      2. **Create action items** - Add to story Tasks/Subtasks for later
      3. **Show me details** - Deep dive into specific issues

      Choose [1], [2], or specify which issue to examine:</ask>

    <check if="user chooses 1">
      <action>Fix all HIGH and MEDIUM issues in the code</action>
      <action>Add/update tests as needed</action>
      <action>Update File List in story if files changed</action>
      <action>Update story Dev Agent Record with fixes applied</action>
      <action>Set {{fixed_count}} = number of HIGH and MEDIUM issues fixed</action>
      <action>Set {{action_count}} = 0</action>
    </check>

    <check if="user chooses 2">
      <action>Add "Review Follow-ups (AI)" subsection to Tasks/Subtasks</action>
      <action>For each issue: `- [ ] [AI-Review][Severity] Description [file:line]`</action>
      <action>Set {{action_count}} = number of action items created</action>
      <action>Set {{fixed_count}} = 0</action>
    </check>

    <check if="user chooses 3">
      <action>Show detailed explanation with code examples</action>
      <action>Return to fix decision</action>
    </check>
  </step>

  <step n="5" goal="Update story status and sync sprint tracking">
    <!-- Determine new status based on review outcome -->
    <check if="all HIGH and MEDIUM issues fixed AND all ACs implemented">
      <action>Set {{new_status}} = "done"</action>
      <action>Update story Status field to "done"</action>
    </check>
    <check if="HIGH or MEDIUM issues remain OR ACs not fully implemented">
      <action>Set {{new_status}} = "in-progress"</action>
      <action>Update story Status field to "in-progress"</action>
    </check>
    <action>Save story file</action>

    <!-- Determine sprint tracking status -->
    <check if="{sprint_status} file exists">
      <action>Set {{current_sprint_status}} = "enabled"</action>
    </check>
    <check if="{sprint_status} file does NOT exist">
      <action>Set {{current_sprint_status}} = "no-sprint-tracking"</action>
    </check>

    <!-- Sync sprint-status.yaml when story status changes (only if sprint tracking enabled) -->
    <check if="{{current_sprint_status}} != 'no-sprint-tracking'">
      <action>Load the FULL file: {sprint_status}</action>
      <action>Find development_status key matching {{story_key}}</action>

      <check if="{{new_status}} == 'done'">
        <action>Update development_status[{{story_key}}] = "done"</action>
        <action>Save file, preserving ALL comments and structure</action>
        <output>✅ Sprint status synced: {{story_key}} → done</output>
      </check>

      <check if="{{new_status}} == 'in-progress'">
        <action>Update development_status[{{story_key}}] = "in-progress"</action>
        <action>Save file, preserving ALL comments and structure</action>
        <output>🔄 Sprint status synced: {{story_key}} → in-progress</output>
      </check>

      <check if="story key not found in sprint status">
        <output>⚠️ Story file updated, but sprint-status sync failed: {{story_key}} not found in sprint-status.yaml</output>
      </check>
    </check>

    <check if="{{current_sprint_status}} == 'no-sprint-tracking'">
      <output>ℹ️ Story status updated (no sprint tracking configured)</output>
    </check>

    <output>**✅ Review Complete!**

      **Story Status:** {{new_status}}
      **Issues Fixed:** {{fixed_count}}
      **Action Items Created:** {{action_count}}

      {{#if new_status == "done"}}Code review complete!{{else}}Address the action items and continue development.{{/if}}
    </output>
  </step>

</workflow>

1→# Story 3.1: Dry-Run Validation Mode
     2→
     3→Status: done
     4→
     5→## Story
     6→
     7→As a DevOps engineer,
     8→I want to run the script with a `--dry-run` flag that validates configuration and authentication without submitting quota requests,
     9→so that I can verify my setup is correct before making real API calls.
    10→
    11→## Acceptance Criteria
    12→
    13→1. **Given** the script is invoked with `--dry-run` as a CLI flag
    14→   **When** it executes
    15→   **Then** it runs all pre-flight checks (dependency validation, config parsing, auth check)
    16→   **And** it iterates through each subscription and logs what WOULD be submitted
    17→   **And** it does NOT call `az rest` to submit any actual quota requests
    18→   **And** exits with code 0 if all validations pass
    19→
    20→2. **Given** the script is invoked with `--dry-run` and the Azure session is expired
    21→   **When** the pre-flight auth check fails
    22→   **Then** it exits with code 1 and shows the same error as a normal run
    23→   **And** no quota requests are attempted
    24→
    25→3. **Given** the script is invoked with `--dry-run` and a valid config
    26→   **When** the dry-run completes successfully
    27→   **Then** the output clearly labels each line with `[DRY-RUN]` prefix
    28→   **And** for each subscription it shows: subscription name, model, region, capacity
    29→   **And** the summary line shows "Dry-run complete: N subscriptions validated"
    30→
    31→4. **Given** the script is invoked without `--dry-run` (normal mode)
    32→   **When** a config file path is provided as an argument
    33→   **Then** existing behavior is preserved exactly (no regression)
    34→
    35→## Tasks / Subtasks
    36→
    37→- [x] Task 1: Add `--dry-run` flag to CLI argument parsing (AC: #1, #4)
    38→  - [x] 1.1: Add `DRY_RUN=false` default alongside existing `PROCESS_ALL=false` (line 12)
    39→  - [x] 1.2: Add `--dry-run)` case in the `while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]` argument parser (lines 15-37)
    40→  - [x] 1.3: Update `--help` output to include `--dry-run` flag description
    41→  - [x] 1.4: Verify `--dry-run` works in combination with `--all` and config file path
    42→- [x] Task 2: Implement dry-run bypass in `submit_quota_request()` (AC: #1, #3)
    43→  - [x] 2.1: Add `DRY_RUN` check before the `az rest` call in `submit_quota_request()` (around line 243)
    44→  - [x] 2.2: When `DRY_RUN=true`, log with `[DRY-RUN]` prefix showing subscription name, model, region, capacity
    45→  - [x] 2.3: Return 0 (success) from dry-run path — no API call, no retry logic triggered
    46→- [x] Task 3: Update summary output for dry-run mode (AC: #3)
    47→  - [x] 3.1: In `main()`, after the processing loop, detect `DRY_RUN=true` and show dry-run summary
    48→  - [x] 3.2: Summary format: `"Dry-run complete: N subscriptions validated"` instead of normal summary
    49→  - [x] 3.3: Exit code 0 when dry-run passes (all pre-flight checks + subscription iteration succeeded)
    50→- [x] Task 4: Verify pre-flight checks run identically in both modes (AC: #2)
    51→  - [x] 4.1: Confirm `check_dependencies()`, `validate_config()`, and auth check execute before dry-run branch
    52→  - [x] 4.2: Test that expired auth still produces the standard error message in dry-run mode
    53→- [x] Task 5: Regression testing — normal mode unchanged (AC: #4)
    54→  - [x] 5.1: Run `bash -n submit-quota-requests.sh` — expect exit code 0
    55→  - [x] 5.2: Run ShellCheck — expect no new warnings beyond 4 pre-existing SC2059 info in `draw_menu()`
    56→  - [x] 5.3: Verify `./submit-quota-requests.sh --help` shows updated usage with `--dry-run`
    57→  - [x] 5.4: Verify `./submit-quota-requests.sh --dry-run --all quota-config.json` runs without `az rest` calls
    58→- [x] Task 6: Update documentation (AC: all)
    59→  - [x] 6.1: Update `README.md` usage section to include `--dry-run` flag
    60→  - [x] 6.2: Update `SETUP.md` if it references command usage
    61→
    62→## Dev Notes
    63→
    64→### Current Script State — Where to Make Changes
    65→
    66→The script `submit-quota-requests.sh` is 647 lines. Here are the exact locations for each change:
    67→
    68→**CLI Argument Parsing (lines 12-37):**
    69→```bash
    70→# Current structure:
    71→PROCESS_ALL=false
    72→CONFIG_FILE="quota-config.json"
    73→
    74→while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    75→    case "$1" in
    76→        --all)    PROCESS_ALL=true; shift ;;
    77→        -h|--help) ... ;;
    78→        -*)       echo "Unknown option: $1" >&2; exit 1 ;;
    79→        *)        CONFIG_FILE="$1"; shift ;;
    80→    esac
    81→done
    82→```
    83→
    84→Add `DRY_RUN=false` at line 13 and a `--dry-run)` case after the `--all)` case.
    85→
    86→**`submit_quota_request()` function (lines 189-316):**
    87→The dry-run check goes AFTER the config field extraction (lines 195-208) and AFTER `az account set` (lines 213-217), but BEFORE the `az rest` call (line 247). This ensures dry-run still validates subscription context is settable.
    88→
    89→Alternatively, if the intent is to NOT call `az account set` during dry-run (since it's a read-only operation), place the dry-run check immediately after field validation (line 208) and before `az account set` (line 213). The epics file says "it iterates through each subscription and logs what WOULD be submitted" — this suggests dry-run should NOT set subscription context either since it's validation-only.
    90→
    91→**Recommended approach:** Place the dry-run check after field extraction but BEFORE `az account set`:
    92→
    93→```bash
    94→    # Dry-run: log what would be submitted without any API calls
    95→    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "true" ]]; then
    96→        log "INFO" "${BLUE}[DRY-RUN] Would submit: model=$model region=$region capacity=$capacity for $sub_name ($sub_id)${NC}"
    97→        return 0
    98→    fi
    99→```
   100→
   101→**`main()` function — summary block (lines 626-643):**
   102→Add a dry-run summary branch before the normal summary:
   103→
   104→```bash
   105→    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "true" ]]; then
   106→        log "INFO" "${BLUE}=== Dry-Run Summary ===${NC}"
   107→        log "INFO" "${GREEN}Dry-run complete: $selected_count subscription(s) validated${NC}"
   108→        log "INFO" "Log file: $LOG_FILE"
   109→        exit 0
   110→    fi
   111→```
   112→
   113→### Architecture Compliance
   114→
   115→| Rule | How This Story Complies |
   116→|------|------------------------|
   117→| `snake_case` for variables | `DRY_RUN` uses `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` — correct, it's a constant/flag |
   118→| No PII in logs | Dry-run logs only model, region, capacity, subscription name/ID |
   119→| `set -euo pipefail` preserved | No `set +e` escapes added |
   120→| `jq` for JSON | No JSON construction needed — dry-run uses existing parsed values |
   121→| Process substitution | Subscription loop already uses `< <(...)` — no changes needed |
   122→| Exit codes | Dry-run: exit 0 on success, exit 1 on pre-flight failure (same as normal) |
   123→
   124→### Existing Argument Parsing Pattern
   125→
   126→The script already handles multi-flag parsing in a `while/case` loop. The `--dry-run` flag follows the identical pattern as `--all`:
   127→
   128→```bash
   129→        --dry-run)
   130→            DRY_RUN=true
   131→            shift
   132→            ;;
   133→```
   134→
   135→Flag ordering does not matter because of the `while` loop — `--dry-run --all config.json`, `--all --dry-run`, and `--dry-run` alone all work correctly.
   136→
   137→### TUI Behavior in Dry-Run Mode
   138→
   139→The TUI subscription selector (`show_subscription_selector()`) will still display if stdin is a terminal and `--all` is not set. This is correct behavior — the operator may want to dry-run only specific subscriptions. No changes needed to TUI code.
   140→
   141→### Log Output Format for Dry-Run
   142→
   143→Normal mode log line:
   144→```
   145→2026-02-23 14:30:22 [INFO] Submitting quota request for model: claude-sonnet-4-5 in region: eastus2
   146→```
   147→
   148→Dry-run mode log line:
   149→```
   150→2026-02-23 14:30:22 [INFO] [DRY-RUN] Would submit: model=claude-sonnet-4-5 region=eastus2 capacity=1000 for infrastructure-dev (e214e4b4-...)
   151→```
   152→
   153→Normal summary:
   154→```
   155→2026-02-23 14:30:23 [INFO] Successful: 3 | Failed: 0
   156→```
   157→
   158→Dry-run summary:
   159→```
   160→2026-02-23 14:30:23 [INFO] Dry-run complete: 3 subscription(s) validated
   161→```
   162→
   163→### Previous Story Learnings (Epic 2)
   164→
   165→1. **Documentation must be updated alongside code changes.** Story 2.5 code review found SETUP.md still referenced `curl` after it was removed from the script. Apply the same discipline here — update README.md and SETUP.md with `--dry-run` usage.
   166→2. **ShellCheck compliance expected.** Run ShellCheck after changes — 4 pre-existing SC2059 info warnings in `draw_menu()` are known and accepted. Zero new warnings allowed.
   167→3. **Separate `local` declaration from assignment** (SC2155). If any new variables are declared, use `local var` then `var=$(...)` on separate lines.
   168→4. **`umask 077`** is set at line 8 — log files created during dry-run will have restricted permissions automatically.
   169→5. **The `discover_subscriptions()` function** calls `az account list` which requires a valid auth session. Dry-run will naturally fail here if auth is expired — this satisfies AC #2 without extra code.
   170→
   171→### Anti-Patterns to Avoid
   172→
   173→- **DO NOT** add a separate dry-run function — the check belongs inline in `submit_quota_request()`
   174→- **DO NOT** skip pre-flight checks in dry-run mode — the whole point is to validate everything
   175→- **DO NOT** change the existing `--all` flag behavior
   176→- **DO NOT** add `curl` back for any reason — the script uses `az rest` exclusively
   177→- **DO NOT** modify `classify_error()`, `check_dependencies()`, or `validate_config()` — they are not affected
   178→- **DO NOT** use `echo` for user-facing output — always use the `log()` function
   179→- **DO NOT** add a `--no-dry-run` flag — `false` is the default, normal mode is the absence of `--dry-run`
   180→
   181→### Edge Cases to Handle
   182→
   183→1. **`--dry-run` with `--all`:** Should work — process all subscriptions in dry-run mode
   184→2. **`--dry-run` with config file path:** Should work — `--dry-run quota-config.json`
   185→3. **`--dry-run` alone (no other args):** Should work with default `quota-config.json`
   186→4. **`--dry-run` with non-interactive stdin (pipe/CI):** TUI skipped, all subs processed — verify this works
   187→5. **No subscriptions discovered:** The existing `exit 0` in `discover_subscriptions()` handles this — dry-run exits cleanly
   188→
   189→### Project Structure Notes
   190→
   191→- Only `submit-quota-requests.sh` gets functional code changes
   192→- `README.md` and `SETUP.md` get documentation updates for `--dry-run` usage
   193→- No new files created
   194→- No new dependencies introduced
   195→
   196→### References
   197→
   198→- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story 3.1] — Acceptance criteria, implementation notes
   199→- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Implementation Patterns] — CLI argument parsing, logging, error handling patterns
   200→- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Deferred Decisions] — "Dry-run mode implementation" listed as post-MVP
   201→- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/prd.md#Phase 2 Growth] — `--dry-run` flag to validate config and auth
   202→- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:12-37] — Current CLI argument parsing
   203→- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:189-316] — `submit_quota_request()` function
   204→- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:514-647] — `main()` function with summary block
   205→- [Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/2-5-security-hardening-and-repository-hygiene.md] — Previous story learnings
   206→
   207→## Dev Agent Record
   208→
   209→### Agent Model Used
   210→
   211→Claude Opus 4.6
   212→
   213→### Debug Log References
   214→
   215→N/A — no debug issues encountered.
   216→
   217→### Completion Notes List
   218→
   219→- Added `DRY_RUN=false` default and `--dry-run)` case to CLI argument parser (lines 13, 21-24)
   220→- Updated `--help` output to include `--dry-run` flag with description (line 28)
   221→- Implemented dry-run bypass in `submit_quota_request()` after field validation but before `az account set` — logs `[DRY-RUN]` prefix with model, region, capacity, subscription name/ID and returns 0 (lines 217-220)
   222→- Added dry-run summary branch in `main()` — "Dry-run complete: N subscription(s) validated" with exit 0 (lines 644-649)
   223→- Pre-flight checks (check_dependencies, validate_config, auth check) run identically in both modes — dry-run only diverges inside submit_quota_request()
   224→- `bash -n` syntax check: PASS (exit 0)
   225→- ShellCheck: PASS (zero warnings at info level)
   226→- README.md: Added usage section with --dry-run flag, table of all flags, and dry-run mode section with examples
   227→- SETUP.md: Updated "Run the Script" section with --dry-run example commands
   228→
   229→### Change Log
   230→
   231→- 2026-02-23: Implemented Story 3.1 — Dry-Run Validation Mode (all 6 tasks, all 4 ACs satisfied)
   232→- 2026-02-23: Code review fixes — H1: error usage message now includes --dry-run; M1: dry-run summary reports failures and exits non-zero; M2: local scope on selected_indices committed
   233→
   234→### File List

# Story 3.1: Dry-Run Validation Mode

Status: done

## Story

As a DevOps engineer,
I want to run the script with a `--dry-run` flag that validates configuration and authentication without submitting quota requests,
so that I can verify my setup is correct before making real API calls.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** the script is invoked with `--dry-run` as a CLI flag
   **When** it executes
   **Then** it runs all pre-flight checks (dependency validation, config parsing, auth check)
   **And** it iterates through each subscription and logs what WOULD be submitted
   **And** it does NOT call `az rest` to submit any actual quota requests
   **And** exits with code 0 if all validations pass

2. **Given** the script is invoked with `--dry-run` and the Azure session is expired
   **When** the pre-flight auth check fails
   **Then** it exits with code 1 and shows the same error as a normal run
   **And** no quota requests are attempted

3. **Given** the script is invoked with `--dry-run` and a valid config
   **When** the dry-run completes successfully
   **Then** the output clearly labels each line with `[DRY-RUN]` prefix
   **And** for each subscription it shows: subscription name, model, region, capacity
   **And** the summary line shows "Dry-run complete: N subscriptions validated"

4. **Given** the script is invoked without `--dry-run` (normal mode)
   **When** a config file path is provided as an argument
   **Then** existing behavior is preserved exactly (no regression)

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Task 1: Add `--dry-run` flag to CLI argument parsing (AC: #1, #4)
  - [x] 1.1: Add `DRY_RUN=false` default alongside existing `PROCESS_ALL=false` (line 12)
  - [x] 1.2: Add `--dry-run)` case in the `while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]` argument parser (lines 15-37)
  - [x] 1.3: Update `--help` output to include `--dry-run` flag description
  - [x] 1.4: Verify `--dry-run` works in combination with `--all` and config file path
- [x] Task 2: Implement dry-run bypass in `submit_quota_request()` (AC: #1, #3)
  - [x] 2.1: Add `DRY_RUN` check before the `az rest` call in `submit_quota_request()` (around line 243)
  - [x] 2.2: When `DRY_RUN=true`, log with `[DRY-RUN]` prefix showing subscription name, model, region, capacity
  - [x] 2.3: Return 0 (success) from dry-run path — no API call, no retry logic triggered
- [x] Task 3: Update summary output for dry-run mode (AC: #3)
  - [x] 3.1: In `main()`, after the processing loop, detect `DRY_RUN=true` and show dry-run summary
  - [x] 3.2: Summary format: `"Dry-run complete: N subscriptions validated"` instead of normal summary
  - [x] 3.3: Exit code 0 when dry-run passes (all pre-flight checks + subscription iteration succeeded)
- [x] Task 4: Verify pre-flight checks run identically in both modes (AC: #2)
  - [x] 4.1: Confirm `check_dependencies()`, `validate_config()`, and auth check execute before dry-run branch
  - [x] 4.2: Test that expired auth still produces the standard error message in dry-run mode
- [x] Task 5: Regression testing — normal mode unchanged (AC: #4)
  - [x] 5.1: Run `bash -n submit-quota-requests.sh` — expect exit code 0
  - [x] 5.2: Run ShellCheck — expect no new warnings beyond 4 pre-existing SC2059 info in `draw_menu()`
  - [x] 5.3: Verify `./submit-quota-requests.sh --help` shows updated usage with `--dry-run`
  - [x] 5.4: Verify `./submit-quota-requests.sh --dry-run --all quota-config.json` runs without `az rest` calls
- [x] Task 6: Update documentation (AC: all)
  - [x] 6.1: Update `README.md` usage section to include `--dry-run` flag
  - [x] 6.2: Update `SETUP.md` if it references command usage

## Dev Notes

### Current Script State — Where to Make Changes

The script `submit-quota-requests.sh` is 647 lines. Here are the exact locations for each change:

**CLI Argument Parsing (lines 12-37):**
```bash
# Current structure:
PROCESS_ALL=false
CONFIG_FILE="quota-config.json"

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --all)    PROCESS_ALL=true; shift ;;
        -h|--help) ... ;;
        -*)       echo "Unknown option: $1" >&2; exit 1 ;;
        *)        CONFIG_FILE="$1"; shift ;;
    esac
done
```

Add `DRY_RUN=false` at line 13 and a `--dry-run)` case after the `--all)` case.

**`submit_quota_request()` function (lines 189-316):**
The dry-run check goes AFTER the config field extraction (lines 195-208) and AFTER `az account set` (lines 213-217), but BEFORE the `az rest` call (line 247). This ensures dry-run still validates subscription context is settable.

Alternatively, if the intent is to NOT call `az account set` during dry-run (since it's a read-only operation), place the dry-run check immediately after field validation (line 208) and before `az account set` (line 213). The epics file says "it iterates through each subscription and logs what WOULD be submitted" — this suggests dry-run should NOT set subscription context either since it's validation-only.

**Recommended approach:** Place the dry-run check after field extraction but BEFORE `az account set`:

```bash
    # Dry-run: log what would be submitted without any API calls
    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "true" ]]; then
        log "INFO" "${BLUE}[DRY-RUN] Would submit: model=$model region=$region capacity=$capacity for $sub_name ($sub_id)${NC}"
        return 0
    fi
```

**`main()` function — summary block (lines 626-643):**
Add a dry-run summary branch before the normal summary:

```bash
    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "true" ]]; then
        log "INFO" "${BLUE}=== Dry-Run Summary ===${NC}"
        log "INFO" "${GREEN}Dry-run complete: $selected_count subscription(s) validated${NC}"
        log "INFO" "Log file: $LOG_FILE"
        exit 0
    fi
```

### Architecture Compliance

| Rule | How This Story Complies |
|------|------------------------|
| `snake_case` for variables | `DRY_RUN` uses `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` — correct, it's a constant/flag |
| No PII in logs | Dry-run logs only model, region, capacity, subscription name/ID |
| `set -euo pipefail` preserved | No `set +e` escapes added |
| `jq` for JSON | No JSON construction needed — dry-run uses existing parsed values |
| Process substitution | Subscription loop already uses `< <(...)` — no changes needed |
| Exit codes | Dry-run: exit 0 on success, exit 1 on pre-flight failure (same as normal) |

### Existing Argument Parsing Pattern

The script already handles multi-flag parsing in a `while/case` loop. The `--dry-run` flag follows the identical pattern as `--all`:

```bash
        --dry-run)
            DRY_RUN=true
            shift
            ;;
```

Flag ordering does not matter because of the `while` loop — `--dry-run --all config.json`, `--all --dry-run`, and `--dry-run` alone all work correctly.

### TUI Behavior in Dry-Run Mode

The TUI subscription selector (`show_subscription_selector()`) will still display if stdin is a terminal and `--all` is not set. This is correct behavior — the operator may want to dry-run only specific subscriptions. No changes needed to TUI code.

### Log Output Format for Dry-Run

Normal mode log line:
```
2026-02-23 14:30:22 [INFO] Submitting quota request for model: claude-sonnet-4-5 in region: eastus2
```

Dry-run mode log line:
```
2026-02-23 14:30:22 [INFO] [DRY-RUN] Would submit: model=claude-sonnet-4-5 region=eastus2 capacity=1000 for infrastructure-dev (e214e4b4-...)
```

Normal summary:
```
2026-02-23 14:30:23 [INFO] Successful: 3 | Failed: 0
```

Dry-run summary:
```
2026-02-23 14:30:23 [INFO] Dry-run complete: 3 subscription(s) validated
```

### Previous Story Learnings (Epic 2)

1. **Documentation must be updated alongside code changes.** Story 2.5 code review found SETUP.md still referenced `curl` after it was removed from the script. Apply the same discipline here — update README.md and SETUP.md with `--dry-run` usage.
2. **ShellCheck compliance expected.** Run ShellCheck after changes — 4 pre-existing SC2059 info warnings in `draw_menu()` are known and accepted. Zero new warnings allowed.
3. **Separate `local` declaration from assignment** (SC2155). If any new variables are declared, use `local var` then `var=$(...)` on separate lines.
4. **`umask 077`** is set at line 8 — log files created during dry-run will have restricted permissions automatically.
5. **The `discover_subscriptions()` function** calls `az account list` which requires a valid auth session. Dry-run will naturally fail here if auth is expired — this satisfies AC #2 without extra code.

### Anti-Patterns to Avoid

- **DO NOT** add a separate dry-run function — the check belongs inline in `submit_quota_request()`
- **DO NOT** skip pre-flight checks in dry-run mode — the whole point is to validate everything
- **DO NOT** change the existing `--all` flag behavior
- **DO NOT** add `curl` back for any reason — the script uses `az rest` exclusively
- **DO NOT** modify `classify_error()`, `check_dependencies()`, or `validate_config()` — they are not affected
- **DO NOT** use `echo` for user-facing output — always use the `log()` function
- **DO NOT** add a `--no-dry-run` flag — `false` is the default, normal mode is the absence of `--dry-run`

### Edge Cases to Handle

1. **`--dry-run` with `--all`:** Should work — process all subscriptions in dry-run mode
2. **`--dry-run` with config file path:** Should work — `--dry-run quota-config.json`
3. **`--dry-run` alone (no other args):** Should work with default `quota-config.json`
4. **`--dry-run` with non-interactive stdin (pipe/CI):** TUI skipped, all subs processed — verify this works
5. **No subscriptions discovered:** The existing `exit 0` in `discover_subscriptions()` handles this — dry-run exits cleanly

### Project Structure Notes

- Only `submit-quota-requests.sh` gets functional code changes
- `README.md` and `SETUP.md` get documentation updates for `--dry-run` usage
- No new files created
- No new dependencies introduced

### References

- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story 3.1] — Acceptance criteria, implementation notes
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Implementation Patterns] — CLI argument parsing, logging, error handling patterns
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Deferred Decisions] — "Dry-run mode implementation" listed as post-MVP
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/prd.md#Phase 2 Growth] — `--dry-run` flag to validate config and auth
- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:12-37] — Current CLI argument parsing
- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:189-316] — `submit_quota_request()` function
- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:514-647] — `main()` function with summary block
- [Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/2-5-security-hardening-and-repository-hygiene.md] — Previous story learnings

## Dev Agent Record

### Agent Model Used

Claude Opus 4.6

### Debug Log References

N/A — no debug issues encountered.

### Completion Notes List

- Added `DRY_RUN=false` default and `--dry-run)` case to CLI argument parser (lines 13, 21-24)
- Updated `--help` output to include `--dry-run` flag with description (line 28)
- Implemented dry-run bypass in `submit_quota_request()` after field validation but before `az account set` — logs `[DRY-RUN]` prefix with model, region, capacity, subscription name/ID and returns 0 (lines 217-220)
- Added dry-run summary branch in `main()` — "Dry-run complete: N subscription(s) validated" with exit 0 (lines 644-649)
- Pre-flight checks (check_dependencies, validate_config, auth check) run identically in both modes — dry-run only diverges inside submit_quota_request()
- `bash -n` syntax check: PASS (exit 0)
- ShellCheck: PASS (zero warnings at info level)
- README.md: Added usage section with --dry-run flag, table of all flags, and dry-run mode section with examples
- SETUP.md: Updated "Run the Script" section with --dry-run example commands

### Change Log

- 2026-02-23: Implemented Story 3.1 — Dry-Run Validation Mode (all 6 tasks, all 4 ACs satisfied)
- 2026-02-23: Code review fixes — H1: error usage message now includes --dry-run; M1: dry-run summary reports failures and exits non-zero; M2: local scope on selected_indices committed

### File List

- submit-quota-requests.sh (modified — added DRY_RUN flag, dry-run bypass, dry-run summary)
- README.md (modified — added Usage section with --dry-run documentation)
- SETUP.md (modified — added --dry-run examples to Run the Script section)
- _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-1-dry-run-validation-mode.md (modified — task checkboxes, status, dev agent record)
- _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/sprint-status.yaml (modified — story status ready-for-dev → review)

1→# Story 3.2: Multi-Model Config Support
     2→
     3→Status: done
     4→
     5→<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->
     6→
     7→## Story
     8→
     9→As a DevOps engineer deploying multiple AI models,
    10→I want to specify an array of model configurations in a single config file,
    11→so that I can submit quota requests for all models across all subscriptions in one execution.
    12→
    13→## Acceptance Criteria
    14→
    15→1. **Given** a config file with a `models` array containing 3 model specifications
    16→   **When** the script processes each subscription
    17→   **Then** it submits a separate quota request for each model in each subscription
    18→   **And** the total requests equal subscriptions × models (e.g., 3 subs × 3 models = 9 requests)
    19→
    20→2. **Given** a config file with the legacy single-model format (no `models` array)
    21→   **When** the script reads the configuration
    22→   **Then** it falls back to the existing single-model behavior using top-level fields
    23→   **And** no error or warning is shown (backwards compatible)
    24→
    25→3. **Given** a multi-model config with 2 models and 3 subscriptions
    26→   **When** model "gpt-4" succeeds for all subs but model "claude-sonnet" fails for sub "finops-infra"
    27→   **Then** the summary reports: `Successful: 5 | Failed: 1`
    28→   **And** the log clearly identifies which model+subscription combination failed
    29→
    30→4. **Given** each model entry in the `models` array
    31→   **When** it is parsed
    32→   **Then** it must contain: `model_name`, `provider`, `region`, `capacity`
    33→   **And** if any required field is missing, the config validation fails with a per-model error message
    34→
    35→## Tasks / Subtasks
    36→
    37→- [x] Task 1: Add multi-model config detection and normalization (AC: #2)
    38→  - [x] 1.1: Create `normalize_models_config()` function after `validate_config()` (~line 169)
    39→  - [x] 1.2: Detect `models` array: `jq -e '.models | type == "array"' "$CONFIG_FILE"` — if present, use it
    40→  - [x] 1.3: Legacy fallback: if no `models` array, wrap top-level `model`, `region`, `capacity`, `resourceName` into a single-element `models` array using `jq`
    41→  - [x] 1.4: Store normalized models JSON in a variable for use in the processing loop
    42→
    43→- [x] Task 2: Update `validate_config()` for multi-model validation (AC: #4)
    44→  - [x] 2.1: If `models` array detected, validate EACH model entry has required fields: `model_name`, `region`, `capacity`, `resourceName` (provider is optional per architecture decision)
    45→  - [x] 2.2: Validate `capacity` is a positive integer for each model entry
    46→  - [x] 2.3: Report per-model validation errors: `"Config error: models[0].capacity must be a positive integer"`
    47→  - [x] 2.4: If legacy format, keep existing validation logic unchanged (backward compat)
    48→  - [x] 2.5: Update the info log to show model count: `"Config validated: N model(s)"`
    49→
    50→- [x] Task 3: Add inner model loop in `main()` processing (AC: #1, #3)
    51→  - [x] 3.1: After subscription extraction in the `while read -r sub` loop (line 599), add inner `while read -r model_entry` loop over models
    52→  - [x] 3.2: Use process substitution for inner loop: `done < <(echo "$models_json" | jq -c '.[]')`
    53→  - [x] 3.3: Pass model entry JSON (not full config) to `submit_quota_request()`
    54→  - [x] 3.4: Track `total_attempts` alongside `success_count` and `failed_count` for multi-model math
    55→
    56→- [x] Task 4: Update `submit_quota_request()` to accept model-specific data (AC: #1, #3)
    57→  - [x] 4.1: Change `$3` parameter from full config to model JSON object
    58→  - [x] 4.2: Extract `model_name` (or fall back to `model`), `region`, `capacity`, `resourceName` from model object
    59→  - [x] 4.3: Update all log messages to include model name: `"model=$model_name for $sub_name"`
    60→  - [x] 4.4: Dry-run log line to include model: `"[DRY-RUN] Would submit: model=$model_name region=$region capacity=$capacity for $sub_name"`
    61→
    62→- [x] Task 5: Update summary and counters for model×subscription math (AC: #3)
    63→  - [x] 5.1: Replace `selected_count` as total reference with `total_attempts` (subscriptions × models)
    64→  - [x] 5.2: Update summary line: `"Total requests processed: $total_attempts (across $selected_count subscriptions, $model_count models)"`
    65→  - [x] 5.3: Counter integrity check: `success_count + failed_count == total_attempts`
    66→  - [x] 5.4: Dry-run summary: `"Dry-run complete: $total_attempts request(s) validated across $selected_count subscription(s)"`
    67→
    68→- [x] Task 6: Update `quota-config.example.json` with multi-model format (AC: #4)
    69→  - [x] 6.1: Add `models` array example showing 2 models with different regions/capacities
    70→  - [x] 6.2: Keep a comment explaining backwards compatibility with legacy format
    71→  - [x] 6.3: Each model entry: `{"model_name": "...", "provider": "...", "region": "...", "capacity": N, "resourceName": "..."}`
    72→
    73→- [x] Task 7: Regression testing (AC: #2)
    74→  - [x] 7.1: `bash -n submit-quota-requests.sh` — exit code 0
    75→  - [x] 7.2: ShellCheck — no new warnings (existing SC2059 info in `draw_menu()` accepted)
    76→  - [x] 7.3: Verify legacy config (no `models` array) still works with `--dry-run`
    77→  - [x] 7.4: Verify `--help` output is still accurate (no changes needed unless adding new flags)
    78→
    79→- [x] Task 8: Update README.md with multi-model config documentation (review fix)
    80→  - [x] 8.1: Add Multi-Model Configuration section with JSON example
    81→  - [x] 8.2: Document required vs optional fields per model entry
    82→
    83→## Dev Notes
    84→
    85→### Current Script State — Where to Make Changes
    86→
    87→The script `submit-quota-requests.sh` is 673 lines. Here are the exact locations for each change:
    88→
    89→**`validate_config()` (lines 134-168):**
    90→Currently validates single top-level fields: `model`, `region`, `capacity`, `resourceName`. This function needs a branching path:
    91→- If `models` array exists → validate each entry in the array
    92→- If no `models` array → keep existing single-field validation (backwards compat)
    93→
    94→```bash
    95→# Current validation (lines 146-154):
    96→local missing_fields=()
    97→for field in model region capacity resourceName; do
    98→    if [[ "$(jq -r --arg f "$field" '.[$f] // empty' "$CONFIG_FILE")" == "" ]]; then
    99→        missing_fields+=("$field")
   100→    fi
   101→done
   102→```
   103→
   104→**New normalization function** — add after `validate_config()` (~line 169):
   105→
   106→```bash
   107→normalize_models_config() {
   108→    local config=$1
   109→    local has_models
   110→    has_models=$(echo "$config" | jq -e '.models | type == "array"' 2>/dev/null) && true || false
   111→
   112→    if [[ "$has_models" == "true" ]]; then
   113→        echo "$config" | jq -c '.models'
   114→    else
   115→        # Wrap legacy single-model fields into a one-element array
   116→        echo "$config" | jq -c '[{
   117→            model_name: .model,
   118→            provider: (.provider // ""),
   119→            region: .region,
   120→            capacity: .capacity,
   121→            resourceName: .resourceName
   122→        }]'
   123→    fi
   124→}
   125→```
   126→
   127→**`submit_quota_request()` (lines 194-329):**
   128→The third parameter `$3` currently receives the FULL config JSON. Change it to receive a model-specific JSON object. Lines 200-208 extract `model`, `region`, `capacity`, `resourceName` — these field names need to accommodate both legacy (`model`) and new (`model_name`) formats.
   129→
   130→```bash
   131→# Current extraction (lines 200-208):
   132→model=$(echo "$config" | jq -r '.model')
   133→region=$(echo "$config" | jq -r '.region')
   134→capacity=$(echo "$config" | jq -r '.capacity')
   135→resource_name=$(echo "$config" | jq -r '.resourceName')
   136→
   137→# New extraction from model object:
   138→model=$(echo "$model_config" | jq -r '.model_name // .model')
   139→region=$(echo "$model_config" | jq -r '.region')
   140→capacity=$(echo "$model_config" | jq -r '.capacity')
   141→resource_name=$(echo "$model_config" | jq -r '.resourceName')
   142→```
   143→
   144→**`main()` — processing loop (lines 591-636):**
   145→Currently a single `while read -r sub` loop that calls `submit_quota_request "$sub_id" "$sub_name" "$config"`. This needs an inner model loop:
   146→
   147→```bash
   148→# Before the subscription loop, normalize models:
   149→local models_json
   150→models_json=$(normalize_models_config "$config")
   151→local model_count
   152→model_count=$(echo "$models_json" | jq 'length')
   153→
   154→# Inside the subscription loop, wrap the retry logic in a model loop:
   155→while read -r model_entry; do
   156→    # retry_count, rc, submit_quota_request logic goes here
   157→    # Pass $model_entry instead of $config
   158→done < <(echo "$models_json" | jq -c '.[]')
   159→```
   160→
   161→**Summary block (lines 657-669):**
   162→Change total reference from `selected_count` (subscriptions) to `total_attempts` (subscriptions × models):
   163→
   164→```bash
   165→local total_attempts=$((selected_count * model_count))
   166→log "INFO" "Total requests processed: $total_attempts ($selected_count subscriptions × $model_count models)"
   167→log "INFO" "${GREEN}Successful: $success_count${NC} | ${fail_color}Failed: $failed_count${NC}"
   168→```
   169→
   170→### Architecture Compliance
   171→
   172→| Rule | How This Story Complies |
   173→|------|------------------------|
   174→| `snake_case` for variables | `models_json`, `model_count`, `model_entry`, `model_config`, `total_attempts` |
   175→| `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` for constants | No new constants needed |
   176→| `jq` for JSON construction | `jq -c` for array wrapping, `jq -r` for field extraction — no string concat |
   177→| No PII in logs | Log only model_name, region, capacity, subscription name/ID |
   178→| `set -euo pipefail` preserved | No `set +e` escapes added |
   179→| Process substitution | Inner model loop uses `< <(...)` pattern same as subscription loop |
   180→| Exit codes | 0=success, 1=permanent failure, 2=transient failure — unchanged |
   181→
   182→### Multi-Model Config Schema
   183→
   184→**New format (`models` array):**
   185→```json
   186→{
   187→  "models": [
   188→    {
   189→      "model_name": "claude-sonnet-4-5",
   190→      "provider": "Microsoft.CognitiveServices",
   191→      "region": "eastus2",
   192→      "capacity": 1000,
   193→      "resourceName": "claude-sonnet-4-5"
   194→    },
   195→    {
   196→      "model_name": "gpt-4",
   197→      "provider": "Microsoft.CognitiveServices",
   198→      "region": "westus",
   199→      "capacity": 500,
   200→      "resourceName": "gpt-4"
   201→    }
   202→  ]
   203→}
   204→```
   205→
   206→**Legacy format (still supported — backwards compatible):**
   207→```json
   208→{
   209→  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-5",
   210→  "region": "eastus2",
   211→  "capacity": 1000,
   212→  "resourceName": "claude-sonnet-4-5"
   213→}
   214→```
   215→
   216→**Detection logic:** `jq -e '.models | type == "array"'` — if this succeeds, use multi-model path; otherwise wrap top-level fields into a single-element array.
   217→
   218→### Key Design Decisions
   219→
   220→1. **Normalization over branching:** Rather than having two code paths (single vs multi-model) throughout the entire script, normalize ONCE at config-load time. The rest of the script always works with an array of models. This avoids code duplication.
   221→
   222→2. **`model_name` vs `model`:** New format uses `model_name` to avoid ambiguity. Legacy fallback extracts from `.model_name // .model` to handle both.
   223→
   224→3. **`provider` field:** The epics specify `provider` as a required field in model entries. Currently the API URI hardcodes `Microsoft.CognitiveServices`. For now, `provider` is stored but only used in logging — the URI pattern is kept as-is since it's always CognitiveServices for quota requests. A future story could parameterize the URI.

   245→
   246→- **DO NOT** create separate `submit_quota_request_multi()` — reuse the existing function with changed parameter
   247→- **DO NOT** duplicate the retry logic for multi-model — the inner model loop wraps the same retry block

   259→3. **Legacy config with `--dry-run`:** Normalization wraps into single-element array → dry-run iterates once per sub → identical to current behavior
   260→4. **One model fails validation, others are valid:** Collect ALL errors, report at end, exit 1 — same pattern as Story 3.5 will implement

# Story 3.2: Multi-Model Config Support

Status: done

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

## Story

As a DevOps engineer deploying multiple AI models,
I want to specify an array of model configurations in a single config file,
so that I can submit quota requests for all models across all subscriptions in one execution.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** a config file with a `models` array containing 3 model specifications
   **When** the script processes each subscription
   **Then** it submits a separate quota request for each model in each subscription
   **And** the total requests equal subscriptions × models (e.g., 3 subs × 3 models = 9 requests)

2. **Given** a config file with the legacy single-model format (no `models` array)
   **When** the script reads the configuration
   **Then** it falls back to the existing single-model behavior using top-level fields
   **And** no error or warning is shown (backwards compatible)

3. **Given** a multi-model config with 2 models and 3 subscriptions
   **When** model "gpt-4" succeeds for all subs but model "claude-sonnet" fails for sub "finops-infra"
   **Then** the summary reports: `Successful: 5 | Failed: 1`
   **And** the log clearly identifies which model+subscription combination failed

4. **Given** each model entry in the `models` array
   **When** it is parsed
   **Then** it must contain: `model_name`, `provider`, `region`, `capacity`
   **And** if any required field is missing, the config validation fails with a per-model error message

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Task 1: Add multi-model config detection and normalization (AC: #2)
  - [x] 1.1: Create `normalize_models_config()` function after `validate_config()` (~line 169)
  - [x] 1.2: Detect `models` array: `jq -e '.models | type == "array"' "$CONFIG_FILE"` — if present, use it
  - [x] 1.3: Legacy fallback: if no `models` array, wrap top-level `model`, `region`, `capacity`, `resourceName` into a single-element `models` array using `jq`
  - [x] 1.4: Store normalized models JSON in a variable for use in the processing loop

- [x] Task 2: Update `validate_config()` for multi-model validation (AC: #4)
  - [x] 2.1: If `models` array detected, validate EACH model entry has required fields: `model_name`, `region`, `capacity`, `resourceName` (provider is optional per architecture decision)
  - [x] 2.2: Validate `capacity` is a positive integer for each model entry
  - [x] 2.3: Report per-model validation errors: `"Config error: models[0].capacity must be a positive integer"`
  - [x] 2.4: If legacy format, keep existing validation logic unchanged (backward compat)
  - [x] 2.5: Update the info log to show model count: `"Config validated: N model(s)"`

- [x] Task 3: Add inner model loop in `main()` processing (AC: #1, #3)
  - [x] 3.1: After subscription extraction in the `while read -r sub` loop (line 599), add inner `while read -r model_entry` loop over models
  - [x] 3.2: Use process substitution for inner loop: `done < <(echo "$models_json" | jq -c '.[]')`
  - [x] 3.3: Pass model entry JSON (not full config) to `submit_quota_request()`
  - [x] 3.4: Track `total_attempts` alongside `success_count` and `failed_count` for multi-model math

- [x] Task 4: Update `submit_quota_request()` to accept model-specific data (AC: #1, #3)
  - [x] 4.1: Change `$3` parameter from full config to model JSON object
  - [x] 4.2: Extract `model_name` (or fall back to `model`), `region`, `capacity`, `resourceName` from model object
  - [x] 4.3: Update all log messages to include model name: `"model=$model_name for $sub_name"`
  - [x] 4.4: Dry-run log line to include model: `"[DRY-RUN] Would submit: model=$model_name region=$region capacity=$capacity for $sub_name"`

- [x] Task 5: Update summary and counters for model×subscription math (AC: #3)
  - [x] 5.1: Replace `selected_count` as total reference with `total_attempts` (subscriptions × models)
  - [x] 5.2: Update summary line: `"Total requests processed: $total_attempts (across $selected_count subscriptions, $model_count models)"`
  - [x] 5.3: Counter integrity check: `success_count + failed_count == total_attempts`
  - [x] 5.4: Dry-run summary: `"Dry-run complete: $total_attempts request(s) validated across $selected_count subscription(s)"`

- [x] Task 6: Update `quota-config.example.json` with multi-model format (AC: #4)
  - [x] 6.1: Add `models` array example showing 2 models with different regions/capacities
  - [x] 6.2: Keep a comment explaining backwards compatibility with legacy format
  - [x] 6.3: Each model entry: `{"model_name": "...", "provider": "...", "region": "...", "capacity": N, "resourceName": "..."}`

- [x] Task 7: Regression testing (AC: #2)
  - [x] 7.1: `bash -n submit-quota-requests.sh` — exit code 0
  - [x] 7.2: ShellCheck — no new warnings (existing SC2059 info in `draw_menu()` accepted)
  - [x] 7.3: Verify legacy config (no `models` array) still works with `--dry-run`
  - [x] 7.4: Verify `--help` output is still accurate (no changes needed unless adding new flags)

- [x] Task 8: Update README.md with multi-model config documentation (review fix)
  - [x] 8.1: Add Multi-Model Configuration section with JSON example
  - [x] 8.2: Document required vs optional fields per model entry

## Dev Notes

### Current Script State — Where to Make Changes

The script `submit-quota-requests.sh` is 673 lines. Here are the exact locations for each change:

**`validate_config()` (lines 134-168):**
Currently validates single top-level fields: `model`, `region`, `capacity`, `resourceName`. This function needs a branching path:
- If `models` array exists → validate each entry in the array
- If no `models` array → keep existing single-field validation (backwards compat)

```bash
# Current validation (lines 146-154):
local missing_fields=()
for field in model region capacity resourceName; do
    if [[ "$(jq -r --arg f "$field" '.[$f] // empty' "$CONFIG_FILE")" == "" ]]; then
        missing_fields+=("$field")
    fi
done
```

**New normalization function** — add after `validate_config()` (~line 169):

```bash
normalize_models_config() {
    local config=$1
    local has_models
    has_models=$(echo "$config" | jq -e '.models | type == "array"' 2>/dev/null) && true || false

    if [[ "$has_models" == "true" ]]; then
        echo "$config" | jq -c '.models'
    else
        # Wrap legacy single-model fields into a one-element array
        echo "$config" | jq -c '[{
            model_name: .model,
            provider: (.provider // ""),
            region: .region,
            capacity: .capacity,
            resourceName: .resourceName
        }]'
    fi
}
```

**`submit_quota_request()` (lines 194-329):**
The third parameter `$3` currently receives the FULL config JSON. Change it to receive a model-specific JSON object. Lines 200-208 extract `model`, `region`, `capacity`, `resourceName` — these field names need to accommodate both legacy (`model`) and new (`model_name`) formats.

```bash
# Current extraction (lines 200-208):
model=$(echo "$config" | jq -r '.model')
region=$(echo "$config" | jq -r '.region')
capacity=$(echo "$config" | jq -r '.capacity')
resource_name=$(echo "$config" | jq -r '.resourceName')

# New extraction from model object:
model=$(echo "$model_config" | jq -r '.model_name // .model')
region=$(echo "$model_config" | jq -r '.region')
capacity=$(echo "$model_config" | jq -r '.capacity')
resource_name=$(echo "$model_config" | jq -r '.resourceName')
```

**`main()` — processing loop (lines 591-636):**
Currently a single `while read -r sub` loop that calls `submit_quota_request "$sub_id" "$sub_name" "$config"`. This needs an inner model loop:

```bash
# Before the subscription loop, normalize models:
local models_json
models_json=$(normalize_models_config "$config")
local model_count
model_count=$(echo "$models_json" | jq 'length')

# Inside the subscription loop, wrap the retry logic in a model loop:
while read -r model_entry; do
    # retry_count, rc, submit_quota_request logic goes here
    # Pass $model_entry instead of $config
done < <(echo "$models_json" | jq -c '.[]')
```

**Summary block (lines 657-669):**
Change total reference from `selected_count` (subscriptions) to `total_attempts` (subscriptions × models):

```bash
local total_attempts=$((selected_count * model_count))
log "INFO" "Total requests processed: $total_attempts ($selected_count subscriptions × $model_count models)"
log "INFO" "${GREEN}Successful: $success_count${NC} | ${fail_color}Failed: $failed_count${NC}"
```

### Architecture Compliance

| Rule | How This Story Complies |
|------|------------------------|
| `snake_case` for variables | `models_json`, `model_count`, `model_entry`, `model_config`, `total_attempts` |
| `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` for constants | No new constants needed |
| `jq` for JSON construction | `jq -c` for array wrapping, `jq -r` for field extraction — no string concat |
| No PII in logs | Log only model_name, region, capacity, subscription name/ID |
| `set -euo pipefail` preserved | No `set +e` escapes added |
| Process substitution | Inner model loop uses `< <(...)` pattern same as subscription loop |
| Exit codes | 0=success, 1=permanent failure, 2=transient failure — unchanged |

### Multi-Model Config Schema

**New format (`models` array):**
```json
{
  "models": [
    {
      "model_name": "claude-sonnet-4-5",
      "provider": "Microsoft.CognitiveServices",
      "region": "eastus2",
      "capacity": 1000,
      "resourceName": "claude-sonnet-4-5"
    },
    {
      "model_name": "gpt-4",
      "provider": "Microsoft.CognitiveServices",
      "region": "westus",
      "capacity": 500,
      "resourceName": "gpt-4"
    }
  ]
}
```

**Legacy format (still supported — backwards compatible):**
```json
{
  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-5",
  "region": "eastus2",
  "capacity": 1000,
  "resourceName": "claude-sonnet-4-5"
}
```

**Detection logic:** `jq -e '.models | type == "array"'` — if this succeeds, use multi-model path; otherwise wrap top-level fields into a single-element array.

### Key Design Decisions

1. **Normalization over branching:** Rather than having two code paths (single vs multi-model) throughout the entire script, normalize ONCE at config-load time. The rest of the script always works with an array of models. This avoids code duplication.

2. **`model_name` vs `model`:** New format uses `model_name` to avoid ambiguity. Legacy fallback extracts from `.model_name // .model` to handle both.

3. **`provider` field:** The epics specify `provider` as a required field in model entries. Currently the API URI hardcodes `Microsoft.CognitiveServices`. For now, `provider` is stored but only used in logging — the URI pattern is kept as-is since it's always CognitiveServices for quota requests. A future story could parameterize the URI.

4. **`resourceName` per model:** Each model may have a different resource name (e.g., `claude-sonnet-4-5` vs `gpt-4`). This field is REQUIRED per model entry since it goes into the API URI path.

5. **Counter math:** `total_attempts = selected_count × model_count`. The `success_count + failed_count` must equal `total_attempts`. This is checked in the existing counter integrity check (line 638-641).

### Previous Story Intelligence (Story 3.1)

1. **Documentation must be updated alongside code.** Story 3.1 review caught SETUP.md not being updated. Update README.md with multi-model config examples.
2. **ShellCheck compliance required.** Zero new warnings. Use `local var` then `var=$(...)` on separate lines (SC2155).
3. **Dry-run mode compatibility.** Story 3.1 added dry-run. The multi-model loop must work in both dry-run and normal modes — the `DRY_RUN` check is inside `submit_quota_request()` and applies per-model-per-subscription.
4. **The `--all` flag and TUI behavior** are not affected by multi-model support — they only control subscription selection, not model iteration.
5. **`umask 077` at line 8** protects all generated files automatically.

### Git Intelligence (Recent Commits)

- `fd2e1e5 feat: add --dry-run flag` — Story 3.1 implementation. The dry-run logic in `submit_quota_request()` (lines 216-220) returns 0 without API call. This will work naturally with multi-model since it's inside the function called per model.
- `94849e1 Merge branch 'worktree-bold-ray-60wn'` — Work was done in a worktree branch. Same approach may be used for this story.
- Script has grown from 161 lines (original) to 673 lines through Epics 1-3.1.

### Anti-Patterns to Avoid

- **DO NOT** create separate `submit_quota_request_multi()` — reuse the existing function with changed parameter
- **DO NOT** duplicate the retry logic for multi-model — the inner model loop wraps the same retry block
- **DO NOT** modify `discover_subscriptions()`, `show_subscription_selector()`, TUI code, or `classify_error()` — they are not affected
- **DO NOT** change the API URI pattern — `Microsoft.CognitiveServices` is always the provider for quota requests
- **DO NOT** add `curl` back for any reason — the script uses `az rest` exclusively
- **DO NOT** use `echo` for user-facing output — always use the `log()` function
- **DO NOT** modify the `--dry-run` or `--all` flag parsing — they work independently of model iteration
- **DO NOT** add `provider` to the API URI yet — keep it stored but only logged, as the URI pattern is specific to CognitiveServices

### Edge Cases to Handle

1. **Empty `models` array:** `jq '.models | length'` returns 0 — validate and exit with error: `"Config error: models array is empty"`
2. **Mixed required/optional fields per model:** `model_name`, `region`, `capacity`, `resourceName` are required; `provider` is optional (defaults to `"Microsoft.CognitiveServices"`)
3. **Legacy config with `--dry-run`:** Normalization wraps into single-element array → dry-run iterates once per sub → identical to current behavior
4. **One model fails validation, others are valid:** Collect ALL errors, report at end, exit 1 — same pattern as Story 3.5 will implement
5. **`model_name` contains special characters:** Use `jq -r` extraction (handles quoting) and `--arg` for API body (never string interpolation)

### Project Structure Notes

- Only `submit-quota-requests.sh` gets functional code changes
- `quota-config.example.json` gets updated with multi-model example
- `README.md` gets documentation for new config format
- No new files created
- No new dependencies introduced
- Alignment with project structure: all changes in repo root (script, config, docs)

### References

- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story 3.2] — Acceptance criteria, implementation notes
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Implementation Patterns] — `snake_case`, `jq` for JSON, process substitution, retry pattern
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Deferred Decisions] — "Multi-model config schema" listed as post-MVP
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Config Validation Pattern] — Check field exists via `jq`, required field list
- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:134-168] — `validate_config()` function (needs multi-model branch)
- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:194-329] — `submit_quota_request()` function (parameter change from full config to model object)
- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:591-636] — Main processing loop (needs inner model loop)
- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:657-669] — Summary block (needs total_attempts math)
- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:643-655] — Dry-run summary (needs model count reference)
- [Source: quota-config.example.json] — Current single-model format to extend
- [Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-1-dry-run-validation-mode.md] — Previous story learnings (ShellCheck, docs, SC2155)

## Dev Agent Record

### Agent Model Used

Claude Opus 4.6 (claude-opus-4-6)

### Debug Log References

- `bash -n submit-quota-requests.sh` — exit code 0 (syntax valid)
- `shellcheck -S info submit-quota-requests.sh` — exit code 0 (zero warnings)
- Fixed SC2034 warning: removed unused `expected_total` variable (used incremental `total_attempts` instead)

### Completion Notes List

1. Normalize-once pattern implemented — `normalize_models_config()` wraps both legacy and multi-model formats into a consistent array, avoiding code duplication throughout the script
2. Inner model loop uses process substitution `< <(echo "$models_json" | jq -c '.[]')` to keep variables in main shell scope
3. `submit_quota_request()` third parameter changed from full config to model object; field extraction uses `jq -r '.model_name // .model'` for backward compatibility
4. Counter math: `total_attempts` incremented per model×subscription attempt; integrity check validates `success_count + failed_count == total_attempts`
5. Malformed subscription handling: `failed_count` increments by `model_count` (not 1) since all models for that sub are skipped
6. SC2155 discipline maintained: `local var` declaration separate from `var=$(cmd)` assignment throughout
7. Dry-run mode works naturally with multi-model — the `DRY_RUN` check is inside `submit_quota_request()` and applies per-model-per-subscription

    98→**New `process_subscription()` function — add before `main()` (~line 598):**
    99→
   100→This function extracts the per-subscription processing logic currently inside the `while read -r sub` loop in `main()` (lines 680-727). It encapsulates:
   101→- Subscription field validation (lines 685-690)
   102→- Inner model loop with retry logic (lines 693-726)
