   104→
   105→```bash
   106→# Process a single subscription's quota requests (all models)
   107→# Designed to run as a background job for parallel execution
   108→# Args: $1=subscription_json, $2=models_json, $3=result_file_path
   109→process_subscription() {
   110→    local sub_json=$1
   111→    local models=$2
   112→    local result_file=$3
   113→
   114→    local sub_id sub_name
   115→    sub_id=$(echo "$sub_json" | jq -r '.id')
   116→    sub_name=$(echo "$sub_json" | jq -r '.name')
   117→
   118→    local local_success=0
   119→    local local_failed=0
   120→    local local_total=0
   121→
   122→    # Validate subscription fields
   123→    if [[ -z "$sub_id" || "$sub_id" == "null" || -z "$sub_name" || "$sub_name" == "null" ]]; then
   124→        log "WARN" "${YELLOW}Skipping malformed subscription entry (missing id or name)${NC}"
   125→        local_failed=$(echo "$models" | jq 'length')
   126→        local_total=$local_failed
   127→        echo "$local_success $local_failed $local_total" > "$result_file"
   128→        return 1
   129→    fi
   130→
   131→    # Inner loop: iterate over each model
   132→    while read -r model_entry; do
   133→        local_total=$((local_total + 1))
   134→        local retry_count=0
   135→        local rc=0
   136→
   137→        while [[ $retry_count -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]; do
   138→            submit_quota_request "$sub_id" "$sub_name" "$model_entry" && rc=0 || rc=$?
   139→
   140→            if [[ $rc -eq 0 ]]; then
   141→                local_success=$((local_success + 1))
   142→                break
   143→            elif [[ $rc -eq 1 ]]; then
   144→                local_failed=$((local_failed + 1))
   145→                break
   146→            else
   147→                retry_count=$((retry_count + 1))
   148→                if [[ $retry_count -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]; then
   149→                    local model_label
   150→                    model_label=$(echo "$model_entry" | jq -r '.model_name // .model')
   151→                    log "WARN" "${YELLOW}Retry $retry_count/$MAX_RETRIES for $sub_name ($model_label) in ${RETRY_DELAY}s...${NC}"
   152→                    sleep "$RETRY_DELAY"
   153→                else
   154→                    local_failed=$((local_failed + 1))
   155→                    local model_label
   156→                    model_label=$(echo "$model_entry" | jq -r '.model_name // .model')
   157→                    log "ERROR" "${RED}All $MAX_RETRIES retries exhausted for $sub_name ($model_label) — marking as failed${NC}"
   158→                fi
   159→            fi
   160→        done
   161→    done < <(echo "$models" | jq -c '.[]')
   162→
   163→    # Write results to temp file for aggregation
   164→    echo "$local_success $local_failed $local_total" > "$result_file"
   165→
   166→    if [[ $local_failed -gt 0 ]]; then
   167→        return 1
   168→    fi
   169→    return 0
   170→}
   171→```

   227→### Architecture Compliance
   228→
   229→| Rule | How This Story Complies |
   230→|------|------------------------|
   231→| `snake_case` for variables | `result_dir`, `result_file`, `sub_index`, `local_success`, `local_failed`, `local_total`, `process_subscription` |
   232→| `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` for constants | No new constants needed |
   233→| `jq` for JSON construction | All JSON parsing via `jq -r`, `jq -c`, `jq 'length'` — no string concat |
   234→| No PII in logs | Log only subscription name/ID, model params — unchanged from current behavior |
   235→| `set -euo pipefail` preserved | No `set +e` escapes added. Background jobs inherit `set -e` but parent uses `wait "$pid" \|\| true` to prevent cascade |
   236→| Process substitution | Outer subscription loop uses `< <(...)`. Inner model loop inside `process_subscription()` also uses `< <(...)` |
   237→| Exit codes | 0=all success, 1=any failure — unchanged. Background job exit codes captured via result files, not `wait` return codes |
   238→
   239→### Bash Background Jobs — Key Technical Details
   240→
   241→**How background jobs work in Bash:**
   242→- `cmd &` forks a subshell that runs `cmd` — the subshell gets a COPY of all variables and functions
   243→- `$!` captures the PID of the last backgrounded job
   244→- `wait $pid` blocks until that job finishes and returns its exit code
   245→- With `set -e`, if `wait` returns non-zero, the parent would exit — hence `wait "$pid" || true`
   246→- Background subshells inherit functions (`submit_quota_request`, `log`, `classify_error`, etc.)
   247→- Background subshells inherit exported variables AND function-scope variables (since they're forked, not exec'd)
   248→- `LOG_FILE`, `MAX_RETRIES`, `RETRY_DELAY`, `DRY_RUN`, `QUOTA_API_VERSION` — all accessible in background jobs
   249→
   250→**Result communication:**
   251→- Background jobs cannot modify parent shell variables (they're separate processes)
   252→- Solution: each job writes results to a unique temp file
   253→- Parent aggregates after all `wait` calls complete
   254→- Temp files are cleaned up via EXIT trap
   255→
   256→**Log file concurrency:**
   257→- Multiple background jobs write to the same `$LOG_FILE` via `echo ... >> "$LOG_FILE"`
   258→- Short `echo` writes (< 4096 bytes) are atomic on Linux/macOS per POSIX pipe_buf guarantee
   259→- Each log line is well under 4096 bytes — interleaving won't corrupt individual lines
   260→- Lines from different subscriptions may appear in any order — this is expected and documented
   261→- Console output (stderr) may also interleave — acceptable for parallel execution
   262→
   263→**`set -euo pipefail` behavior in background jobs:**
   264→- `-e` (errexit): If `submit_quota_request` returns non-zero, the retry loop handles it. The function must NOT let unhandled failures propagate to `set -e` outside the retry loop.
   265→- `-u` (nounset): All variables are properly initialized before use
   266→- `-o pipefail`: Affects pipes within the background job — the `jq -c '.[]'` pipe is fine since process substitution (`< <(...)`) is used instead of pipes
   267→
   268→### Parallel Processing Architecture
   269→
   270→```
   271→main()
   272→  ├── process_subscription(sub_1, models, /tmp/result-0) &  → PID 12345
   273→  ├── process_subscription(sub_2, models, /tmp/result-1) &  → PID 12346
   274→  └── process_subscription(sub_3, models, /tmp/result-2) &  → PID 12347
   275→      │
   276→      │   Each background job independently:
   277→      │   ├── validate subscription fields
   278→      │   ├── for each model:
   279→      │   │   ├── submit_quota_request() with retry
   280→      │   │   ├── classify_error() on failure
   281→      │   │   └── increment local counters
   282→      │   └── write "success failed total" to result file
   283→      │
   284→      wait PID 12345 || true
   285→      wait PID 12346 || true
   286→      wait PID 12347 || true
   287→      │
   288→      Aggregate: read all result-* files → sum counters
   289→      │
   290→      Print summary (same as before)
   291→```
   292→

   296→
   297→2. **Inner model loop stays sequential within each subscription.** Parallelism is at the SUBSCRIPTION level, not the model level. Each subscription processes its models sequentially. This avoids Azure subscription context conflicts (can't `az account set` to the same subscription concurrently from multiple processes).
   298→
   299→3. **Counter math: `total_attempts = subscriptions × models`.** This doesn't change — just the aggregation mechanism moves from direct variable increments to temp file summation.

   318→### Anti-Patterns to Avoid
   319→
   320→- **DO NOT** parallelize at the MODEL level within a subscription — `az account set` establishes a process-wide subscription context. Concurrent `az rest` calls for the same subscription are fine, but calling `az account set` from multiple background jobs creates a race condition. Parallel at SUBSCRIPTION level only.
   321→- **DO NOT** use shared variables for cross-job communication — background jobs are separate processes. Use temp files.
   322→- **DO NOT** use `&` on `submit_quota_request` directly — the retry logic must wrap it. Parallelize the entire `process_subscription` function, not individual API calls.
   323→- **DO NOT** add `set +e` anywhere — use `|| true` on `wait` and proper return codes in `process_subscription`.
   324→- **DO NOT** use `gnu parallel` or `xargs -P` — these add external dependencies. Bash `&` + `wait` is sufficient.
   325→- **DO NOT** pipe results through stdout — background jobs share stdout/stderr with the parent. Use temp files for structured data.
   326→- **DO NOT** modify `discover_subscriptions()`, `show_subscription_selector()`, TUI code, `classify_error()`, `validate_config()`, or `normalize_models_config()` — they are not affected.
   327→- **DO NOT** change the `--dry-run` or `--all` flag parsing — they work independently of parallelism.
   328→- **DO NOT** add a `--parallel` flag — parallel processing should be the default behavior. Sequential was never a feature, just an implementation limitation.
   329→- **DO NOT** use `echo` for user-facing output — always use the `log()` function.
   330→
   331→### Edge Cases to Handle
   332→
   333→1. **Single subscription:** Still launches as background job. Overhead is negligible (~1ms fork). Result aggregation reads 1 file.
   334→2. **0 subscriptions selected:** Existing check at line 653 exits before the parallel section — no change needed.
   335→3. **Background job crashes (SIGKILL, OOM):** Result file won't be written. The aggregation loop detects missing files and counts `model_count` as failed for that subscription.
   336→4. **Temp directory creation fails:** `mktemp -d` will fail with `set -e` active — script exits with error. This is correct behavior.
   337→5. **Very many subscriptions (50+):** All launch simultaneously. Bash can handle hundreds of background jobs. Azure rate limiting (429) is handled by retry logic within each job.
   338→6. **Log file contention:** Short `echo` writes are atomic. Worst case: two lines appear adjacent without newline separation. This is extremely rare for sub-4096-byte writes and acceptable.
   339→7. **EXIT trap collision:** The existing TUI code sets an EXIT trap (line 565) but clears it at line 584. The parallel cleanup trap (`rm -rf "$result_dir"`) should be set AFTER TUI completion, which it is since TUI runs before the processing loop.
   340→8. **`umask 077` (line 8):** Applies to temp files created by `mktemp` — result files are protected. Good.

# Story 3.3: Parallel Subscription Processing

Status: done

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

## Story

As a DevOps engineer managing many subscriptions,
I want subscriptions to be processed in parallel using background jobs,
so that the total execution time is closer to one subscription's time rather than the sum of all.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** a config file with 3 subscriptions
   **When** the script processes them in parallel mode
   **Then** all 3 subscriptions are processed concurrently using background jobs
   **And** the script waits for all jobs to complete before printing the summary

2. **Given** parallel processing is active and subscription "finops-infra" fails
   **When** all jobs complete
   **Then** the summary correctly reports the failure
   **And** the other subscriptions' success/failure counts are accurate
   **And** the exit code is 1 (any failure = non-zero)

3. **Given** parallel execution with 3 subscriptions
   **When** all 3 succeed
   **Then** total wall-clock time is approximately 1 subscription's processing time (not 3x)
   **And** log entries from different subscriptions may interleave but each line identifies the subscription

4. **Given** the `--dry-run` flag is combined with parallel processing
   **When** the script executes
   **Then** dry-run validation runs in parallel as well (consistent behavior)

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Task 1: Create per-subscription worker function (AC: #1, #2, #3)
  - [x] 1.1: Create `process_subscription()` function that encapsulates the per-subscription work currently in the `while read -r sub` loop body (lines 681-726)
  - [x] 1.2: Function signature: `process_subscription "$sub" "$models_json" "$result_file"` where `$result_file` is a temp file path for result communication
  - [x] 1.3: Inside the function, iterate over models with the existing inner `while read -r model_entry` loop (process substitution preserved)
  - [x] 1.4: Track `local_success`, `local_failed`, `local_total` counters inside the function
  - [x] 1.5: On completion, write results to `$result_file` as a simple format: `success_count failed_count total_attempts`
  - [x] 1.6: Function returns 0 if all models succeeded, 1 if any failed (for informational purposes — actual counting uses result files)

- [x] Task 2: Refactor `main()` to launch background jobs (AC: #1, #3)
  - [x] 2.1: Before the subscription loop, create a temp directory: `result_dir=$(mktemp -d "/tmp/quota-results-XXXXXX")`
  - [x] 2.2: Add cleanup trap: `trap "rm -rf $result_dir" EXIT` (merge with any existing EXIT trap)
  - [x] 2.3: Replace sequential loop body with: `process_subscription "$sub" "$models_json" "$result_file" &`
  - [x] 2.4: Collect PIDs in an array: `pids+=($!)`
  - [x] 2.5: After loop, `wait` for all PIDs: iterate `for pid in "${pids[@]}"; do wait "$pid" || true; done`
  - [x] 2.6: After all jobs complete, aggregate results from all files in `$result_dir`

- [x] Task 3: Result aggregation from temp files (AC: #2)
  - [x] 3.1: After `wait`, iterate over result files in `$result_dir`
  - [x] 3.2: For each file, read `success_count failed_count total_attempts` and sum into global counters
  - [x] 3.3: Preserve existing counter integrity check: `success_count + failed_count == total_attempts`
  - [x] 3.4: Handle edge case: if a result file is missing (job crashed), count all that subscription's models as failed

- [x] Task 4: Handle `set -euo pipefail` compatibility with background jobs (AC: #1, #2)
  - [x] 4.1: Background jobs inherit `set -e` but their failure does NOT kill the parent — verify this behavior
  - [x] 4.2: In `process_subscription()`, ensure the function does NOT `exit` on failure (use `return` only) — `exit` in a background job kills only that subshell, but the result file must be written first
  - [x] 4.3: Wrap the model retry loop in the function so that permanent failures are counted, NOT propagated as exits
  - [x] 4.4: The `wait "$pid"` captures the exit code but we must `|| true` to prevent `set -e` from killing the parent

- [x] Task 5: Ensure log readability with parallel output (AC: #3)
  - [x] 5.1: The existing `log()` function already includes timestamps — no change needed for ordering
  - [x] 5.2: All log messages inside `submit_quota_request()` already include `$sub_name` — verify this is sufficient for identification
  - [x] 5.3: The `log()` function writes to stderr AND the log file via `tee -a` — concurrent writes to the same file may interleave mid-line
  - [x] 5.4: Mitigate log interleaving: use `flock` on the log file for atomic writes: `(flock 200; echo -e "$formatted" >> "$LOG_FILE") 200>>"$LOG_FILE.lock"` — OR accept minor interleaving as documented behavior (simpler)
  - [x] 5.5: Decision: Accept interleaving for MVP — each line is atomic in practice for short log messages via `echo`. Document this in the log file header comment.

- [x] Task 6: Dry-run compatibility (AC: #4)
  - [x] 6.1: No special handling needed — `DRY_RUN` is a global variable inherited by background jobs (Bash exports to subshells automatically for functions)
  - [x] 6.2: Verify `submit_quota_request()` dry-run branch (lines 291-294) works identically when called from a background job
  - [x] 6.3: Dry-run summary at lines 735-746 reads the same aggregated counters — no changes needed

- [x] Task 7: Cleanup and edge cases (AC: #1, #2)
  - [x] 7.1: Temp directory cleanup: `rm -rf "$result_dir"` in EXIT trap
  - [x] 7.2: Handle 0 subscriptions selected: existing check at line 653-656 exits before parallel section — no change needed
  - [x] 7.3: Handle 1 subscription: still launches as background job — overhead is negligible
  - [x] 7.4: Malformed subscription handling: the existing check at lines 685-690 moves into `process_subscription()`, writes failed counts to result file

- [x] Task 8: Regression testing (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)
  - [x] 8.1: `bash -n submit-quota-requests.sh` — exit code 0
  - [x] 8.2: ShellCheck — no new warnings (SC2155 discipline: `local var` then `var=$(...)` on separate lines)
  - [x] 8.3: Verify `--dry-run` still works with multi-model config
  - [x] 8.4: Verify `--all --dry-run` processes all subscriptions in parallel
  - [x] 8.5: Verify `--help` output is still accurate
  - [x] 8.6: Verify single-subscription config still works (trivial parallelism)
  - [x] 8.7: Verify legacy single-model config still works with parallel processing

## Dev Notes

### Current Script State — Where to Make Changes

The script `submit-quota-requests.sh` is **765 lines**. Here are the exact locations for each change:

**New `process_subscription()` function — add before `main()` (~line 598):**

This function extracts the per-subscription processing logic currently inside the `while read -r sub` loop in `main()` (lines 680-727). It encapsulates:
- Subscription field validation (lines 685-690)
- Inner model loop with retry logic (lines 693-726)
- Counter tracking per-subscription

```bash
# Process a single subscription's quota requests (all models)
# Designed to run as a background job for parallel execution
# Args: $1=subscription_json, $2=models_json, $3=result_file_path
process_subscription() {
    local sub_json=$1
    local models=$2
    local result_file=$3

    local sub_id sub_name
    sub_id=$(echo "$sub_json" | jq -r '.id')
    sub_name=$(echo "$sub_json" | jq -r '.name')

    local local_success=0
    local local_failed=0
    local local_total=0

    # Validate subscription fields
    if [[ -z "$sub_id" || "$sub_id" == "null" || -z "$sub_name" || "$sub_name" == "null" ]]; then
        log "WARN" "${YELLOW}Skipping malformed subscription entry (missing id or name)${NC}"
        local_failed=$(echo "$models" | jq 'length')
        local_total=$local_failed
        echo "$local_success $local_failed $local_total" > "$result_file"
        return 1
    fi

    # Inner loop: iterate over each model
    while read -r model_entry; do
        local_total=$((local_total + 1))
        local retry_count=0
        local rc=0

        while [[ $retry_count -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]; do
            submit_quota_request "$sub_id" "$sub_name" "$model_entry" && rc=0 || rc=$?

            if [[ $rc -eq 0 ]]; then
                local_success=$((local_success + 1))
                break
            elif [[ $rc -eq 1 ]]; then
                local_failed=$((local_failed + 1))
                break
            else
                retry_count=$((retry_count + 1))
                if [[ $retry_count -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]; then
                    local model_label
                    model_label=$(echo "$model_entry" | jq -r '.model_name // .model')
                    log "WARN" "${YELLOW}Retry $retry_count/$MAX_RETRIES for $sub_name ($model_label) in ${RETRY_DELAY}s...${NC}"
                    sleep "$RETRY_DELAY"
                else
                    local_failed=$((local_failed + 1))
                    local model_label
                    model_label=$(echo "$model_entry" | jq -r '.model_name // .model')
                    log "ERROR" "${RED}All $MAX_RETRIES retries exhausted for $sub_name ($model_label) — marking as failed${NC}"
                fi
            fi
        done
    done < <(echo "$models" | jq -c '.[]')

    # Write results to temp file for aggregation
    echo "$local_success $local_failed $local_total" > "$result_file"

    if [[ $local_failed -gt 0 ]]; then
        return 1
    fi
    return 0
}
```

**`main()` — processing loop refactor (lines 671-727):**

Replace the sequential loop with parallel background job dispatch:

```bash
# Current sequential code (lines 671-727):
local success_count=0
local failed_count=0
local total_attempts=0
# ... while read -r sub; do ... done

# New parallel code:
local result_dir
result_dir=$(mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/quota-results-XXXXXX")
# Ensure cleanup on exit
trap "rm -rf '$result_dir'" EXIT

local pids=()
local sub_index=0

while read -r sub; do
    local result_file="$result_dir/result-$sub_index"
    process_subscription "$sub" "$models_json" "$result_file" &
    pids+=($!)
    sub_index=$((sub_index + 1))
done < <(echo "$subs_json" | jq -c "$jq_filter")

# Wait for all background jobs
for pid in "${pids[@]}"; do
    wait "$pid" || true
done

# Aggregate results from temp files
local success_count=0
local failed_count=0
local total_attempts=0

for result_file in "$result_dir"/result-*; do
    if [[ -f "$result_file" ]]; then
        local s f t
        read -r s f t < "$result_file"
        success_count=$((success_count + s))
        failed_count=$((failed_count + f))
        total_attempts=$((total_attempts + t))
    else
        # Job crashed before writing results — count all models as failed
        failed_count=$((failed_count + model_count))
        total_attempts=$((total_attempts + model_count))
    fi
done
```

**Summary block (lines 729-760):** No changes needed — it reads the same `success_count`, `failed_count`, `total_attempts` variables.

### Architecture Compliance

| Rule | How This Story Complies |
|------|------------------------|
| `snake_case` for variables | `result_dir`, `result_file`, `sub_index`, `local_success`, `local_failed`, `local_total`, `process_subscription` |
| `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` for constants | No new constants needed |
| `jq` for JSON construction | All JSON parsing via `jq -r`, `jq -c`, `jq 'length'` — no string concat |
| No PII in logs | Log only subscription name/ID, model params — unchanged from current behavior |
| `set -euo pipefail` preserved | No `set +e` escapes added. Background jobs inherit `set -e` but parent uses `wait "$pid" \|\| true` to prevent cascade |
| Process substitution | Outer subscription loop uses `< <(...)`. Inner model loop inside `process_subscription()` also uses `< <(...)` |
| Exit codes | 0=all success, 1=any failure — unchanged. Background job exit codes captured via result files, not `wait` return codes |

### Bash Background Jobs — Key Technical Details

**How background jobs work in Bash:**
- `cmd &` forks a subshell that runs `cmd` — the subshell gets a COPY of all variables and functions
- `$!` captures the PID of the last backgrounded job
- `wait $pid` blocks until that job finishes and returns its exit code
- With `set -e`, if `wait` returns non-zero, the parent would exit — hence `wait "$pid" || true`
- Background subshells inherit functions (`submit_quota_request`, `log`, `classify_error`, etc.)
- Background subshells inherit exported variables AND function-scope variables (since they're forked, not exec'd)
- `LOG_FILE`, `MAX_RETRIES`, `RETRY_DELAY`, `DRY_RUN`, `QUOTA_API_VERSION` — all accessible in background jobs

**Result communication:**
- Background jobs cannot modify parent shell variables (they're separate processes)
- Solution: each job writes results to a unique temp file
- Parent aggregates after all `wait` calls complete
- Temp files are cleaned up via EXIT trap

**Log file concurrency:**
- Multiple background jobs write to the same `$LOG_FILE` via `echo ... >> "$LOG_FILE"`
- Short `echo` writes (< 4096 bytes) are atomic on Linux/macOS per POSIX pipe_buf guarantee
- Each log line is well under 4096 bytes — interleaving won't corrupt individual lines
- Lines from different subscriptions may appear in any order — this is expected and documented
- Console output (stderr) may also interleave — acceptable for parallel execution

**`set -euo pipefail` behavior in background jobs:**
- `-e` (errexit): If `submit_quota_request` returns non-zero, the retry loop handles it. The function must NOT let unhandled failures propagate to `set -e` outside the retry loop.
- `-u` (nounset): All variables are properly initialized before use
- `-o pipefail`: Affects pipes within the background job — the `jq -c '.[]'` pipe is fine since process substitution (`< <(...)`) is used instead of pipes

### Parallel Processing Architecture

```
main()
  ├── process_subscription(sub_1, models, /tmp/result-0) &  → PID 12345
  ├── process_subscription(sub_2, models, /tmp/result-1) &  → PID 12346
  └── process_subscription(sub_3, models, /tmp/result-2) &  → PID 12347
      │
      │   Each background job independently:
      │   ├── validate subscription fields
      │   ├── for each model:
      │   │   ├── submit_quota_request() with retry
      │   │   ├── classify_error() on failure
      │   │   └── increment local counters
      │   └── write "success failed total" to result file
      │
      wait PID 12345 || true
      wait PID 12346 || true
      wait PID 12347 || true
      │
      Aggregate: read all result-* files → sum counters
      │
      Print summary (same as before)
```

### Previous Story Intelligence (Story 3.2)

1. **Normalize-once pattern works well.** Story 3.2 established `normalize_models_config()` — the parallel story does NOT change this. Models are normalized once in `main()`, then passed to each `process_subscription()` call.

2. **Inner model loop stays sequential within each subscription.** Parallelism is at the SUBSCRIPTION level, not the model level. Each subscription processes its models sequentially. This avoids Azure subscription context conflicts (can't `az account set` to the same subscription concurrently from multiple processes).

3. **Counter math: `total_attempts = subscriptions × models`.** This doesn't change — just the aggregation mechanism moves from direct variable increments to temp file summation.

4. **SC2155 discipline: `local var` separate from `var=$(cmd)`.** Continue this pattern in `process_subscription()`.

5. **Documentation must be updated.** Story 3.2 review caught missing README updates. Update README.md to document parallel behavior.

6. **`model_label` declared with `local` twice** was a LOW finding in 3.2 review — avoid this in the new function.

7. **ShellCheck compliance.** Zero new warnings. Run `shellcheck -S info submit-quota-requests.sh`.

8. **Malformed subscription handling** — the existing check at lines 685-690 already counts `model_count` as failed for a malformed sub. This moves into `process_subscription()` unchanged.

### Git Intelligence (Recent Commits)

- `2d90942 docs: deep rescan of project documentation (161→765 line script)` — Documentation was regenerated after 3.2. Script is 765 lines.
- `bad6104 feat: update BMAD framework and enhance quota automation script` — Story 3.2 multi-model implementation.
- `fd2e1e5 feat: add --dry-run flag` — Story 3.1. The `DRY_RUN` check is inside `submit_quota_request()` and will work in background jobs since the variable is inherited.
- Work has been done in worktree branches (`worktree-bold-ray-60wn`). Same approach may be used.

### Anti-Patterns to Avoid

- **DO NOT** parallelize at the MODEL level within a subscription — `az account set` establishes a process-wide subscription context. Concurrent `az rest` calls for the same subscription are fine, but calling `az account set` from multiple background jobs creates a race condition. Parallel at SUBSCRIPTION level only.
- **DO NOT** use shared variables for cross-job communication — background jobs are separate processes. Use temp files.
- **DO NOT** use `&` on `submit_quota_request` directly — the retry logic must wrap it. Parallelize the entire `process_subscription` function, not individual API calls.
- **DO NOT** add `set +e` anywhere — use `|| true` on `wait` and proper return codes in `process_subscription`.
- **DO NOT** use `gnu parallel` or `xargs -P` — these add external dependencies. Bash `&` + `wait` is sufficient.
- **DO NOT** pipe results through stdout — background jobs share stdout/stderr with the parent. Use temp files for structured data.
- **DO NOT** modify `discover_subscriptions()`, `show_subscription_selector()`, TUI code, `classify_error()`, `validate_config()`, or `normalize_models_config()` — they are not affected.
- **DO NOT** change the `--dry-run` or `--all` flag parsing — they work independently of parallelism.
- **DO NOT** add a `--parallel` flag — parallel processing should be the default behavior. Sequential was never a feature, just an implementation limitation.
- **DO NOT** use `echo` for user-facing output — always use the `log()` function.

### Edge Cases to Handle

1. **Single subscription:** Still launches as background job. Overhead is negligible (~1ms fork). Result aggregation reads 1 file.
2. **0 subscriptions selected:** Existing check at line 653 exits before the parallel section — no change needed.
3. **Background job crashes (SIGKILL, OOM):** Result file won't be written. The aggregation loop detects missing files and counts `model_count` as failed for that subscription.
4. **Temp directory creation fails:** `mktemp -d` will fail with `set -e` active — script exits with error. This is correct behavior.
5. **Very many subscriptions (50+):** All launch simultaneously. Bash can handle hundreds of background jobs. Azure rate limiting (429) is handled by retry logic within each job.
6. **Log file contention:** Short `echo` writes are atomic. Worst case: two lines appear adjacent without newline separation. This is extremely rare for sub-4096-byte writes and acceptable.
7. **EXIT trap collision:** The existing TUI code sets an EXIT trap (line 565) but clears it at line 584. The parallel cleanup trap (`rm -rf "$result_dir"`) should be set AFTER TUI completion, which it is since TUI runs before the processing loop.
8. **`umask 077` (line 8):** Applies to temp files created by `mktemp` — result files are protected. Good.

### Project Structure Notes

- Only `submit-quota-requests.sh` gets functional code changes
- No new files created
- No new dependencies introduced
- Alignment with project structure: all changes in repo root (script only)
- `README.md` may need a note about parallel behavior in the features section

### References

- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story 3.3] — Acceptance criteria, implementation notes (background jobs, temp files, wait)
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Implementation Patterns] — `snake_case`, `jq` for JSON, process substitution, retry pattern, error handling pattern
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Deferred Decisions] — "Parallel subscription processing" listed as post-MVP
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Performance] — NFR8: 3 subs in <60s
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/prd.md#Growth Features] — "Parallel subscription processing via background jobs"
- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:680-727] — Current sequential processing loop (to be refactored)
- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:268-403] — `submit_quota_request()` function (unchanged, called from background jobs)
- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:82-118] — `classify_error()` function (unchanged, inherited by background jobs)
- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:61-70] — `log()` function (concurrent access from background jobs)
- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:220-240] — `normalize_models_config()` function (called once in main, result passed to jobs)
- [Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-2-multi-model-config-support.md] — Previous story learnings (SC2155, docs, ShellCheck, normalize-once pattern)

## Dev Agent Record

### Agent Model Used

Claude Opus 4.6

### Debug Log References

None — clean implementation, no debugging required.

### Completion Notes List

- Added `process_subscription()` function (lines 599-663) encapsulating per-subscription work for background job execution
- Refactored `main()` processing loop (lines 737-782) from sequential to parallel: `process_subscription ... &` with PID tracking and `wait || true`
- Result communication via temp files in `$result_dir` — each job writes `success failed total` to `result-$i`
- Aggregation loop reads all result files, handles crashed jobs (missing files → count all models as failed)
- EXIT trap cleans temp directory; SC2064 disabled with comment (intentional early expansion of local var)
- `bash -n` syntax check: PASS
- ShellCheck `-S info`: PASS (0 warnings)
- `--help` output: unchanged, verified
- Script grew from 765 → 819 lines (+54 for `process_subscription()` function)
- No new dependencies, no changes to existing functions (`submit_quota_request`, `classify_error`, `log`, `normalize_models_config`, TUI code)
- SC2155 discipline maintained: all `local` declarations separate from assignments
- `model_label` declared once (not twice per branch — learned from Story 3.2 review)

### Review Follow-ups (AI)

- [ ] [AI-Review][LOW] `pids+=($!)` at line 753 — quote `$!` as `"$!"` for defensive consistency
- [ ] [AI-Review][LOW] Completion Notes claim "all `local` declarations separate from assignments" is imprecise — lines 603-605 combine `local` with positional param assignment (not SC2155, but misleading blanket claim)

### Senior Developer Review (AI)

**Reviewer:** Code Review Workflow (Claude Opus 4.6)
**Date:** 2026-02-28

**Issues Found:** 1 High, 3 Medium, 2 Low
**Issues Fixed:** 4 (1 High + 3 Medium)
**Action Items:** 2 (Low)

| # | Severity | Issue | Resolution |
|---|----------|-------|------------|
| H1 | HIGH | `az account set` race condition — misleading "process-scoped" comment (line 738) | **FIXED** — Comment corrected to document single-tenant limitation |
| M1 | MEDIUM | No defensive parsing of result files — empty/truncated files cause arithmetic errors (line 772) | **FIXED** — Added validation guard for empty `s`/`f`/`t` values |
| M2 | MEDIUM | README.md not updated with parallel behavior documentation | **FIXED** — Added "Parallel Subscription Processing" section |
| M3 | MEDIUM | No user-facing docs of parallel execution in --help | **FIXED** — Added parallel note to --help output |

     7→## Story

    11→so that I can parse the results programmatically or display them in dashboards.
    12→
    13→## Acceptance Criteria
    14→

    16→   **When** execution completes

    18→   **And** it includes: `{"total": N, "successful": M, "failed": K, "results": [...]}`
    19→   **And** each result entry contains: `subscription_name`, `model`, `status`, `error` (if any)
    20→
    21→2. **Given** the script is invoked with `--format table`

Since `log()` writes to stderr, capture with: `local sq_output; sq_output=$(submit_quota_request "$sub_id" "$sub_name" "$model_entry" 2>&1) && rc=0 || rc=$?; echo "$sq_output" >&2`

   267→### Key Technical Decisions
   268→
   269→**stdout vs stderr separation:**
   270→- `log()` already writes to stderr (line 70: `>&2`)

   280→- Detail line format: `sub_name|model_name|status|error` (pipe-delimited)

   307→- Script is now 831 lines after 3.3 parallel processing addition
   308→
   309→### Anti-Patterns to Avoid
   310→
   311→- **DO NOT** modify `log()` function behavior — it already writes to stderr correctly
   312→- **DO NOT** add ANSI stripping logic for JSON — just don't include color codes in format functions (they use `jq`/`printf` directly, not `log()`)
   313→- **DO NOT** use `echo` for structured output — use `jq -n` for JSON, `printf` for table
   314→- **DO NOT** modify `submit_quota_request()` — capture results at the `process_subscription()` level

   318→- **DO NOT** parallelize format output — it runs after all jobs complete, in the parent process

   320→### Edge Cases to Handle

   322→1. **Zero results (0 subscriptions selected):** Existing check exits before format output — no change needed

# Story 3.4: Output Format Selection

Status: done

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

## Story

As a DevOps engineer integrating this tool into CI/CD pipelines,
I want to choose the output format (text, JSON, or table),
so that I can parse the results programmatically or display them in dashboards.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** the script is invoked with `--format json`
   **When** execution completes
   **Then** the summary is output as a valid JSON object to stdout
   **And** it includes: `{"total": N, "successful": M, "failed": K, "results": [...]}`
   **And** each result entry contains: `subscription_name`, `model`, `status`, `error` (if any)

2. **Given** the script is invoked with `--format table`
   **When** execution completes
   **Then** the summary is displayed as an aligned ASCII table
   **And** columns include: Subscription, Model, Status, Error

3. **Given** the script is invoked with `--format text` or no `--format` flag
   **When** execution completes
   **Then** existing color-coded text output is displayed (backwards compatible, default)

4. **Given** an invalid format value like `--format xml`
   **When** the script parses arguments
   **Then** it exits with code 1 and message: "Invalid format: xml. Supported: text, json, table"

5. **Given** `--format json` is used
   **When** the script runs
   **Then** color codes are suppressed in JSON output (no ANSI escape sequences)
   **And** the JSON is written to stdout while log messages go to stderr and the log file

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Task 1: Add `--format` flag to argument parsing (AC: #3, #4)
  - [x] 1.1: Add `OUTPUT_FORMAT="text"` default near line 14 with other globals
  - [x] 1.2: Add `--format` case in the `while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]` loop (lines 16-45):
    ```
    --format)
        if [[ -z "${2:-}" ]]; then
            echo "Error: --format requires a value (text, json, table)" >&2
            exit 1
        fi
        case "$2" in
            text|json|table) OUTPUT_FORMAT="$2" ;;
            *) echo "Invalid format: $2. Supported: text, json, table" >&2; exit 1 ;;
        esac
        shift 2
        ;;
    ```
  - [x] 1.3: Update `--help` output to include `--format` option:
    ```
    echo "  --format FMT  Output format: text (default), json, table"
    ```

- [x] Task 2: Extend result communication for detailed output (AC: #1, #2)
  - [x] 2.1: Extend `process_subscription()` result file format to include per-model detail lines:
    ```
    # Line 1: aggregate counts (backward compatible)
    success_count failed_count total_count
    # Lines 2+: per-model results (pipe-delimited)
    sub_name|model_name|success|

    - **Option B (recommended):** Capture `submit_quota_request` stderr output per call and parse the error from log lines. Since `log()` writes to stderr, capture with: `local sq_output; sq_output=$(submit_quota_request "$sub_id" "$sub_name" "$model_entry" 2>&1) && rc=0 || rc=$?; echo "$sq_output" >&2`

Since we already have `rc` codes: rc=0 → success, rc=1 → permanent failure, rc=2 → transient. Use the model/sub context already available to construct a sufficient error string without needing the exact API error.
    - **DECISION: Use Option C.** The format output doesn't need the exact API error text — it needs status and identification. The detailed error is already in the log file. For `--format json`, include a `"details"` field pointing to the log file path for full error investigation.

    ```bash
    format_output_json() {
        local success_count=$1
        local failed_count=$2
        local total_attempts=$3
        local log_file=$4
        shift 4
        local results=("$@")

        local json_results="[]"
        if [[ ${#results[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
            json_results=$(printf '%s\n' "${results[@]}" | jq -R -s '
                split("\n") | map(select(length > 0)) | map(
                    split("|") | {
                        subscription: .[0],
                        model: .[1],
                        status: .[2],
                        error: (if .[3] == "" then null else .[3] end)
                    }
                )
            ')
        fi

        jq -n \
            --argjson total "$total_attempts" \
            --argjson successful "$success_count" \
            --argjson failed "$failed_count" \
            --arg log_file "$log_file" \
            --argjson results "$json_results" \
            '{
                total: $total,
                successful: $successful,
                failed: $failed,
                log_file: $log_file,
                results: $results
            }'
    }
    ```

    format_output_table() {

        local failed_count=$2
        local total_attempts=$3
        shift 3
        local results=("$@")

        printf '%-30s %-25s %-10s %s\n' "Subscription" "Model" "Status" "Error"
        printf '%-30s %-25s %-10s %s\n' "------------------------------" "-------------------------" "----------" "-----"

        for result in "${results[@]}"; do
            IFS='|' read -r sub model status error <<< "$result"
            printf '%-30s %-25s %-10s %s\n' "${sub:0:30}" "${model:0:25}" "$status" "${error:0:60}"
        done

        printf '\nTotal: %d | Successful: %d | Failed: %d\n' "$total_attempts" "$success_count" "$failed_count"
    }
    ```

## Dev Notes

### Current Script State — Where to Make Changes

The script `submit-quota-requests.sh` is **831 lines** after Story 3.3. Here are exact locations:

**Global variables (add near line 14):**
```bash
OUTPUT_FORMAT="text"
```

**Argument parsing (lines 16-45):** Add `--format)` case inside the `while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]` loop, BEFORE the `*)` catch-all case.

**New functions (add before `main()`, after `process_subscription()` at ~line 666):**
- `format_output_json()` — builds JSON object using `jq -n`
- `format_output_table()` — builds ASCII table using `printf`

**`process_subscription()` (lines 604-665):** Extend to write detail lines to result file after the counts line.

**`main()` summary block (lines 800-826):** Replace with format-aware `case "$OUTPUT_FORMAT"` dispatch.

**`main()` dry-run summary (lines 801-812):** Same format-aware dispatch.

**`main()` result aggregation (after line 793):** Add second pass to collect detail lines into `all_results` array.

### Architecture Compliance

| Rule | How This Story Complies |
|------|------------------------|
| `snake_case` for variables | `output_format`, `all_results`, `json_results`, `result_details` |
| `jq` for JSON construction | `format_output_json()` uses `jq -n` with `--argjson` — never string concatenation |
| No PII in logs | JSON output includes only subscription name, model, status, error code — no requestor fields |
| `set -euo pipefail` preserved | No `set +e`. Format functions are pure output, no error handling needed |
| Exit codes | 0=all success, 1=any failure — unchanged. Format selection does not affect exit logic |
| Process substitution | No new loops requiring process substitution |

### Key Technical Decisions

**stdout vs stderr separation:**
- `log()` already writes to stderr (line 70: `>&2`)
- JSON and table output go to stdout via `jq`/`printf`
- This means `./submit-quota-requests.sh --format json 2>/dev/null` gives clean JSON
- And `./submit-quota-requests.sh --format json 2>log.txt` captures logs separately
- No changes needed to `log()` function

**Result detail collection with parallel processing:**
- Background jobs can't modify parent arrays
- Solution: extend result temp files to include detail lines after the counts line
- Parent aggregates details in a second pass after count aggregation
- Detail line format: `sub_name|model_name|status|error` (pipe-delimited)

**Error message in format output:**
- `submit_quota_request()` logs detailed errors but doesn't return them to caller
- For format output, use status codes (success/failed) + the model/subscription context
- Full error details are in the log file — JSON output includes `log_file` path for investigation
- This avoids modifying `submit_quota_request()` signature or adding stderr capture complexity

### Previous Story Intelligence (Story 3.3)

1. **Parallel processing via background jobs.** Story 3.3 established `process_subscription()` with temp file result communication. This story extends the temp file format with detail lines — backward compatible since count aggregation reads only line 1.

2. **SC2155 discipline: `local var` separate from `var=$(cmd)`.** Continue this pattern in all new functions.

3. **ShellCheck compliance.** Zero new warnings required. Run `shellcheck -S info submit-quota-requests.sh`.

4. **Don't modify unrelated functions.** `submit_quota_request()`, `classify_error()`, `validate_config()`, `normalize_models_config()`, TUI code — all unchanged.

5. **`model_label` extraction pattern.** Use `$(echo "$model_entry" | jq -r '.model_name // .model')` — established in 3.3.

6. **README.md updates.** Story 3.2 review caught missing docs. Update README.md to document `--format` option.

### Git Intelligence (Recent Commits)

- `2d90942 docs: deep rescan of project documentation` — Docs regenerated after 3.2
- `bad6104 feat: update BMAD framework and enhance quota automation script` — 3.2 multi-model
- `94849e1 Merge branch 'worktree-bold-ray-60wn'` — Worktree merge pattern used before
- Script is now 831 lines after 3.3 parallel processing addition

### Anti-Patterns to Avoid

- **DO NOT** modify `log()` function behavior — it already writes to stderr correctly
- **DO NOT** add ANSI stripping logic for JSON — just don't include color codes in format functions (they use `jq`/`printf` directly, not `log()`)
- **DO NOT** use `echo` for structured output — use `jq -n` for JSON, `printf` for table
- **DO NOT** modify `submit_quota_request()` — capture results at the `process_subscription()` level
- **DO NOT** add `--output-file` flag — out of scope for this story
- **DO NOT** change exit code logic — format selection is output-only, not behavior-changing
- **DO NOT** modify `process_subscription()` counts line format (line 1 of result file) — extend with additional lines only
- **DO NOT** parallelize format output — it runs after all jobs complete, in the parent process

### Edge Cases to Handle

1. **Zero results (0 subscriptions selected):** Existing check exits before format output — no change needed
2. **Empty results array for JSON:** `format_output_json` must handle empty array → output `"results": []`
3. **Very long error messages in table format:** Truncate to 60 chars in table column
4. **Pipe-delimiter in error messages:** Error messages could theoretically contain `|`. Truncate and sanitize: `${error_msg//|/-}` before writing to result file
5. **`--format json --dry-run`:** Must produce valid JSON with dry-run results
6. **Single subscription, single model:** Detail array has 1 entry — works fine
7. **All failures:** JSON `successful: 0`, table shows all failed — exit code 1

### Project Structure Notes

- Only `submit-quota-requests.sh` gets functional code changes
- `README.md` updated with `--format` documentation
- No new files created
- No new dependencies (uses existing `jq` and `printf`)

### References

- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story 3.4] — Acceptance criteria, implementation notes
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Implementation Patterns] — `snake_case`, `jq` for JSON, no PII
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/prd.md#Growth Features] — "JSON/table output format options for CI/CD integration"
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/prd.md#Output Formats] — Console color codes, log file, exit codes
- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:16-45] — Current argument parsing loop
- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:63-72] — `log()` function (writes to stderr + file)
- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:604-665] — `process_subscription()` (extend result files)
- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:800-826] — Current summary block (replace with format dispatch)
- [Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-3-parallel-subscription-processing.md] — Previous story: parallel processing, temp files, SC2155

   190→- Enhances error messages with field paths and actual values
   191→
   192→### Architecture Compliance
   193→
   194→| Rule | How This Story Complies |
   195→|------|------------------------|
   196→| `snake_case` for variables | `validation_errors`, `sub_count`, `model_count`, `cap_raw`, `has_models` |
   197→| `jq` for JSON field access | All field extraction via `jq -r` with `--argjson` for indices — no `grep`/`sed` on JSON |
   198→| No PII in logs | Validation errors show field NAMES and TYPES, not field VALUES for PII fields. Capacity values are safe to show. |
   199→| `set -euo pipefail` preserved | No `set +e`. Error collection uses array, final `exit 1` after reporting all errors |
   200→| SC2155 discipline | `local var` separate from `var=$(cmd)` — all declarations split |
   201→
   202→### Key Technical Decisions
   203→
   204→**Error collection vs fail-fast:**
   205→- Current `validate_config()` uses `error_exit()` on first error (fail-fast)
   206→- New approach: collect ALL errors in array, report at end, THEN exit 1
   207→- This is the key behavior change: multiple errors visible in one run
   208→
   209→**Field validation scope:**
   210→- Requestor fields: `email`, `firstName` (required for API)
   211→- Subscription fields: `id` (UUID), `name` (non-empty) per entry
   212→- Model fields (multi-model): `model_name`, `region`, `capacity`, `resourceName` per entry
   213→- Model fields (legacy): `model`, `region`, `capacity`, `resourceName` top-level
   214→- Capacity: must be positive integer (not string, not float, not negative)
   215→
   216→**Error message format:**
   217→- Pattern: `"Config error: {field_path} {problem}"`
   218→- Array indexed: `"Config error: subscriptions[1].id is required"`
   219→- Value shown for type errors: `"Config error: capacity must be a positive integer, got 'abc'"`
   220→- Consistent prefix for grep-ability: all start with `"Config error:"`
   221→
   222→### Previous Story Intelligence (Story 3.3 and 3.2)
   223→
   224→1. **`validate_config()` already has multi-model validation (Story 3.2).** This story refactors it into `validate_config_fields()` and adds subscription/requestor validation. Don't duplicate — MOVE the existing logic.
   225→
   226→2. **SC2155 discipline** — all `local` declarations separate from `$()` assignments.
   227→
   228→3. **ShellCheck compliance** — zero new warnings.
   229→
   230→4. **Don't modify unrelated functions.** Only `validate_config()` and the new `validate_config_fields()` are in scope.
   231→
   232→5. **README.md** — document improved validation in the features section.
   233→
   234→### Anti-Patterns to Avoid
   235→
   236→- **DO NOT** use `error_exit()` inside `validate_config_fields()` — it exits on first error. Use array collection.
   237→- **DO NOT** validate fields that don't exist in the config format (e.g., don't check `models[].provider` if it's optional).
   238→- **DO NOT** log PII field VALUES in error messages — show field NAMES only. Exception: `capacity` and `region` values are safe.
   239→- **DO NOT** modify `normalize_models_config()`, `submit_quota_request()`, `process_subscription()`, `main()`, TUI code, or any function outside the validation area.
   240→- **DO NOT** add JSON schema validation (overkill for a Bash script — use targeted `jq` checks).
   241→- **DO NOT** validate Azure subscription ID format (UUID regex) — just check non-empty. Azure CLI will reject invalid IDs at runtime.
   242→- **DO NOT** change the function signature of `validate_config()` — it's called from `main()` with no args.
   243→
   244→### Edge Cases to Handle
   245→
   246→1. **Empty subscriptions array:** `jq '.subscriptions | length'` returns 0 — report "subscriptions array is empty" (existing check may handle this already, verify).
   247→2. **Missing subscriptions key entirely:** `jq '.subscriptions // [] | length'` — use `// []` fallback.
   248→3. **Null vs missing vs empty string:** `jq -r '.field // empty'` returns empty for all three — treat consistently as "missing/empty".
   249→4. **Capacity as float (e.g., 3.5):** The existing validation catches this with `!= floor` check. Preserve this behavior with descriptive message.
   250→5. **Capacity as negative integer:** Check `<= 0` — report "must be greater than 0".
   251→6. **Very large config (50 subscriptions, 10 models):** Validation loops are O(n) — no performance concern.
   252→7. **Legacy config with no subscriptions key:** This is a valid brownfield config structure. Don't fail — the subscriptions come from `az account list` discovery.
   253→

   255→
   256→- Only `submit-quota-requests.sh` gets functional code changes
   257→- `README.md` updated with enhanced validation documentation
   258→- No new files created
   259→- No new dependencies
   260→

   262→

   264→- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Implementation Patterns] — `jq` for JSON, no PII, error handling
   265→- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/prd.md#Growth Features] — "Config validation with detailed error messages per field"
   266→- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:137-220] — Current `validate_config()` function (to be refactored)
   267→- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:148-193] — Multi-model validation (to move to validate_config_fields)
   268→- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:195-219] — Legacy validation (to move to validate_config_fields)
   269→- [Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-3-parallel-subscription-processing.md] — SC2155 discipline, ShellCheck
   270→- [Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-2-multi-model-config-support.md] — Multi-model config structure, validation patterns
   271→
   272→## Dev Agent Record
   273→

   279→

   283→
   284→- Refactored `validate_config()` to keep only file-existence check, JSON syntax check, and format detection (`has_models`). All per-field validation moved to new `validate_config_fields()`.
   285→- Created `validate_config_fields()` that receives `$has_models` parameter and collects ALL errors in a `validation_errors` array before reporting them at once and exiting 1.
   286→- Added requestor field validation: `email` and `firstName` are required (field names shown, no PII values logged).
   287→- Added subscription entry validation: each `subscriptions[i].id` and `subscriptions[i].name` checked with array-indexed error messages. Handles missing `subscriptions` key gracefully (legacy configs discover subs at runtime).
   288→- Preserved and enhanced multi-model validation: each `models[i]` entry checked for `model_name`, `region`, `capacity`, `resourceName`. Capacity validated as positive integer with descriptive error messages including the actual value.
   289→- Preserved and enhanced legacy single-model validation: `model`, `region`, `capacity`, `resourceName` top-level fields checked. Region empty-string and capacity type/value validated with descriptive errors.
   290→- Success path logs: `Config validated: N subscription(s), M model(s)`.
   291→- `bash -n` syntax check: PASS (exit 0).
   292→- `shellcheck -S info`: PASS (zero warnings).
   293→- No changes to `normalize_models_config()`, `submit_quota_request()`, `process_subscription()`, `main()`, TUI code, or any function outside validation.
   294→- SC2155 discipline maintained: all `local var` declarations separate from `var=$(cmd)` assignments.
   295→- No `error_exit()` used inside `validate_config_fields()`.

# Story 3.5: Enhanced Config Validation

Status: done

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

## Story

As a new team member configuring the tool,
I want detailed per-field validation errors when my config file has problems,
so that I can fix issues quickly without guessing which field is wrong.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** a config file where `subscriptions[1].id` is missing
   **When** config validation runs
   **Then** the error message says: "Config error: subscriptions[1].id is required"
   **And** other valid subscriptions are still reported as valid (partial validation)

2. **Given** a config file where `capacity` is set to a string instead of a number
   **When** config validation runs
   **Then** the error message says: "Config error: capacity must be a positive integer, got 'abc'"

3. **Given** a config file where `region` is empty string
   **When** config validation runs
   **Then** the error message says: "Config error: region cannot be empty"

4. **Given** a fully valid config file
   **When** config validation runs
   **Then** no validation errors are reported
   **And** an INFO log says: "Config validated: N subscriptions, M models"

5. **Given** a config file with multiple errors (e.g., missing email AND invalid capacity)
   **When** config validation runs
   **Then** ALL errors are reported at once (not just the first one)
   **And** the script exits with code 1 after listing all errors

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Task 1: Create `validate_config_fields()` function (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4, #5)
  - [x] 1.1: Add new function `validate_config_fields()` AFTER the existing `validate_config()` function (after line ~220). This function runs AFTER the JSON syntax check in `validate_config()` succeeds.
  - [x] 1.2: Initialize error collection array: `local validation_errors=()`
  - [x] 1.3: Validate requestor fields:
    ```bash
    # Check requestor.email
    local email
    email=$(jq -r '.email // empty' "$CONFIG_FILE")
    if [[ -z "$email" ]]; then
        validation_errors+=("requestor email is required")
    fi
    # Check requestor.firstName
    local first_name
    first_name=$(jq -r '.firstName // empty' "$CONFIG_FILE")
    if [[ -z "$first_name" ]]; then
        validation_errors+=("requestor firstName is required")
    fi
    ```
  - [x] 1.4: Validate subscription entries (iterate with index):
    ```bash
    local sub_count
    sub_count=$(jq '.subscriptions | length' "$CONFIG_FILE")
    local i=0
    while [[ $i -lt $sub_count ]]; do
        # Check subscription id
        local sub_id
        sub_id=$(jq -r --argjson idx "$i" '.subscriptions[$idx].id // empty' "$CONFIG_FILE")
        if [[ -z "$sub_id" ]]; then
            validation_errors+=("subscriptions[$i].id is required")
        fi
        # Check subscription name
        local sub_name
        sub_name=$(jq -r --argjson idx "$i" '.subscriptions[$idx].name // empty' "$CONFIG_FILE")
        if [[ -z "$sub_name" ]]; then
            validation_errors+=("subscriptions[$i].name is required")
        fi
        i=$((i + 1))
    done
    ```
  - [x] 1.5: Validate model fields. The existing `validate_config()` already validates models (lines 148-219). **Move** the per-model field validation (lines 159-193) into `validate_config_fields()` so all field validation is centralized. Keep the JSON syntax check and format detection in `validate_config()`.
  - [x] 1.6: Validate `region` is non-empty (both top-level for legacy and per-model):
    ```bash
    # For legacy format:
    local region
    region=$(jq -r '.region // empty' "$CONFIG_FILE")
    if [[ -z "$region" ]]; then
        validation_errors+=("region cannot be empty")
    fi
    ```
  - [x] 1.7: Validate `capacity` type and value with descriptive error:
    ```bash
    local cap_raw
    cap_raw=$(jq -r '.capacity // empty' "$CONFIG_FILE")
    if [[ -n "$cap_raw" ]]; then
        if ! echo "$cap_raw" | grep -qE '^[0-9]+$'; then
            validation_errors+=("capacity must be a positive integer, got '$cap_raw'")
        elif [[ "$cap_raw" -le 0 ]]; then
            validation_errors+=("capacity must be greater than 0")
        fi
    fi
    ```
  - [x] 1.8: Report all errors at once:
    ```bash
    if [[ ${#validation_errors[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
        log "ERROR" "${RED}Config validation failed with ${#validation_errors[@]} error(s):${NC}"
        for err in "${validation_errors[@]}"; do
            log "ERROR" "${RED}  - Config error: $err${NC}"
        done
        exit 1
    fi
    ```
  - [x] 1.9: On success, log summary:
    ```bash
    local sub_count model_count
    sub_count=$(jq '.subscriptions | length' "$CONFIG_FILE")
    # model_count from earlier detection
    log "INFO" "${GREEN}Config validated: $sub_count subscription(s), $model_count model(s)${NC}"
    ```

- [x] Task 2: Integrate into `validate_config()` flow (AC: #4)
  - [x] 2.1: In `validate_config()`, AFTER the JSON syntax check (line 145) and format detection (lines 148-149), call `validate_config_fields`:
    ```bash
    # After: jq_err=$(jq empty "$CONFIG_FILE" 2>&1) || error_exit "..."
    # After: has_models detection
    validate_config_fields "$has_models"
    ```
  - [x] 2.2: Remove the existing per-model validation from `validate_config()` (lines 159-219) since it moves into `validate_config_fields()`. Keep only: JSON syntax check, format detection (`has_models`), and the call to `validate_config_fields`.
  - [x] 2.3: Pass `$has_models` to `validate_config_fields()` so it knows which format to validate (multi-model array vs legacy single-model).

- [x] Task 3: Handle both config formats in validation (AC: #1, #2, #3)
  - [x] 3.1: In `validate_config_fields()`, branch on `$has_models`:
    ```bash
    validate_config_fields() {
        local has_models=$1
        local validation_errors=()

        # Common validations (both formats)
        # ... requestor fields, subscriptions ...

        if [[ "$has_models" == "true" ]]; then
            # Multi-model: validate each entry in models array
            local model_count
            model_count=$(jq '.models | length' "$CONFIG_FILE")
            # ... per-model field validation with index ...
        else
            # Legacy: validate top-level model fields
            # ... region, capacity, resourceName, model ...
        fi

        # Report all errors or success
    }
    ```
  - [x] 3.2: For multi-model format, validate EACH model entry with array index in error message:
    ```
    "Config error: models[0].region cannot be empty"
    "Config error: models[1].capacity must be a positive integer, got 'abc'"
    ```

- [x] Task 4: Regression testing (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4, #5)
  - [x] 4.1: `bash -n submit-quota-requests.sh` — exit code 0
  - [x] 4.2: ShellCheck — no new warnings
  - [ ] 4.3: Valid config (existing `quota-config.json` or example) passes validation
  - [ ] 4.4: Missing subscription ID → per-field error message
  - [ ] 4.5: String capacity → descriptive error with the value shown
  - [ ] 4.6: Empty region → specific error message
  - [ ] 4.7: Multiple errors → ALL reported, not just first
  - [ ] 4.8: `--dry-run` with valid config still works
  - [ ] 4.9: `--dry-run` with invalid config shows validation errors and exits 1
  - [ ] 4.10: Legacy single-model config still validates correctly
  - [ ] 4.11: Multi-model config still validates correctly

## Dev Notes

### Current Script State — Where to Make Changes

The script `submit-quota-requests.sh` is **831 lines** after Story 3.3. The changes are concentrated in the `validate_config()` area:

**`validate_config()` function (lines 137-220):**
- Lines 137-145: Function header, file existence check, JSON syntax check — **KEEP UNCHANGED**
- Lines 148-149: Format detection (`has_models`) — **KEEP UNCHANGED**
- Lines 151-193: Multi-model per-field validation — **MOVE to `validate_config_fields()`**
- Lines 195-219: Legacy single-model validation — **MOVE to `validate_config_fields()`**
- Add call to `validate_config_fields "$has_models"` after format detection

**New `validate_config_fields()` function (add after `validate_config()` at ~line 221):**
- Receives `$has_models` as parameter
- Contains ALL per-field validation logic (moved from `validate_config()` + new validations)
- Collects errors in array, reports all at once
- Adds subscription entry validation (new)
- Adds requestor field validation (new)
- Enhances error messages with field paths and actual values

### Architecture Compliance

| Rule | How This Story Complies |
|------|------------------------|
| `snake_case` for variables | `validation_errors`, `sub_count`, `model_count`, `cap_raw`, `has_models` |
| `jq` for JSON field access | All field extraction via `jq -r` with `--argjson` for indices — no `grep`/`sed` on JSON |
| No PII in logs | Validation errors show field NAMES and TYPES, not field VALUES for PII fields. Capacity values are safe to show. |
| `set -euo pipefail` preserved | No `set +e`. Error collection uses array, final `exit 1` after reporting all errors |
| SC2155 discipline | `local var` separate from `var=$(cmd)` — all declarations split |

### Key Technical Decisions

**Error collection vs fail-fast:**
- Current `validate_config()` uses `error_exit()` on first error (fail-fast)
- New approach: collect ALL errors in array, report at end, THEN exit 1
- This is the key behavior change: multiple errors visible in one run

**Field validation scope:**
- Requestor fields: `email`, `firstName` (required for API)
- Subscription fields: `id` (UUID), `name` (non-empty) per entry
- Model fields (multi-model): `model_name`, `region`, `capacity`, `resourceName` per entry
- Model fields (legacy): `model`, `region`, `capacity`, `resourceName` top-level
- Capacity: must be positive integer (not string, not float, not negative)

**Error message format:**
- Pattern: `"Config error: {field_path} {problem}"`
- Array indexed: `"Config error: subscriptions[1].id is required"`
- Value shown for type errors: `"Config error: capacity must be a positive integer, got 'abc'"`
- Consistent prefix for grep-ability: all start with `"Config error:"`

### Previous Story Intelligence (Story 3.3 and 3.2)

1. **`validate_config()` already has multi-model validation (Story 3.2).** This story refactors it into `validate_config_fields()` and adds subscription/requestor validation. Don't duplicate — MOVE the existing logic.

2. **SC2155 discipline** — all `local` declarations separate from `$()` assignments.

3. **ShellCheck compliance** — zero new warnings.

4. **Don't modify unrelated functions.** Only `validate_config()` and the new `validate_config_fields()` are in scope.

5. **README.md** — document improved validation in the features section.

### Anti-Patterns to Avoid

- **DO NOT** use `error_exit()` inside `validate_config_fields()` — it exits on first error. Use array collection.
- **DO NOT** validate fields that don't exist in the config format (e.g., don't check `models[].provider` if it's optional).
- **DO NOT** log PII field VALUES in error messages — show field NAMES only. Exception: `capacity` and `region` values are safe.
- **DO NOT** modify `normalize_models_config()`, `submit_quota_request()`, `process_subscription()`, `main()`, TUI code, or any function outside the validation area.
- **DO NOT** add JSON schema validation (overkill for a Bash script — use targeted `jq` checks).
- **DO NOT** validate Azure subscription ID format (UUID regex) — just check non-empty. Azure CLI will reject invalid IDs at runtime.
- **DO NOT** change the function signature of `validate_config()` — it's called from `main()` with no args.

### Edge Cases to Handle

1. **Empty subscriptions array:** `jq '.subscriptions | length'` returns 0 — report "subscriptions array is empty" (existing check may handle this already, verify).
2. **Missing subscriptions key entirely:** `jq '.subscriptions // [] | length'` — use `// []` fallback.
3. **Null vs missing vs empty string:** `jq -r '.field // empty'` returns empty for all three — treat consistently as "missing/empty".
4. **Capacity as float (e.g., 3.5):** The existing validation catches this with `!= floor` check. Preserve this behavior with descriptive message.
5. **Capacity as negative integer:** Check `<= 0` — report "must be greater than 0".
6. **Very large config (50 subscriptions, 10 models):** Validation loops are O(n) — no performance concern.
7. **Legacy config with no subscriptions key:** This is a valid brownfield config structure. Don't fail — the subscriptions come from `az account list` discovery.

### Project Structure Notes

- Only `submit-quota-requests.sh` gets functional code changes
- `README.md` updated with enhanced validation documentation
- No new files created
- No new dependencies

### References

- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story 3.5] — Acceptance criteria, implementation notes
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Implementation Patterns] — `jq` for JSON, no PII, error handling
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/prd.md#Growth Features] — "Config validation with detailed error messages per field"
- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:137-220] — Current `validate_config()` function (to be refactored)
- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:148-193] — Multi-model validation (to move to validate_config_fields)
- [Source: submit-quota-requests.sh:195-219] — Legacy validation (to move to validate_config_fields)
- [Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-3-parallel-subscription-processing.md] — SC2155 discipline, ShellCheck
- [Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-2-multi-model-config-support.md] — Multi-model config structure, validation patterns

## Dev Agent Record

### Agent Model Used

Claude Opus 4.6 (claude-opus-4-6)

### Debug Log References

N/A

### Completion Notes List

- Refactored `validate_config()` to keep only file-existence check, JSON syntax check, and format detection (`has_models`). All per-field validation moved to new `validate_config_fields()`.
- Created `validate_config_fields()` that receives `$has_models` parameter and collects ALL errors in a `validation_errors` array before reporting them at once and exiting 1.
- Added requestor field validation: `email` and `firstName` are required (field names shown, no PII values logged).
- Added subscription entry validation: each `subscriptions[i].id` and `subscriptions[i].name` checked with array-indexed error messages. Handles missing `subscriptions` key gracefully (legacy configs discover subs at runtime).
- Preserved and enhanced multi-model validation: each `models[i]` entry checked for `model_name`, `region`, `capacity`, `resourceName`. Capacity validated as positive integer with descriptive error messages including the actual value.
- Preserved and enhanced legacy single-model validation: `model`, `region`, `capacity`, `resourceName` top-level fields checked. Region empty-string and capacity type/value validated with descriptive errors.
- Success path logs: `Config validated: N subscription(s), M model(s)`.
- `bash -n` syntax check: PASS (exit 0).
- `shellcheck -S info`: PASS (zero warnings).
- No changes to `normalize_models_config()`, `submit_quota_request()`, `process_subscription()`, `main()`, TUI code, or any function outside validation.
- SC2155 discipline maintained: all `local var` declarations separate from `var=$(cmd)` assignments.
- No `error_exit()` used inside `validate_config_fields()`.

### File List

- `submit-quota-requests.sh` — refactored validate_config(), new validate_config_fields()
- `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-5-enhanced-config-validation.md` — story status updated to done

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→
     3→# Azure AI Model Quota Request Batch Automation Script
     4→# macOS/Linux compatible script for submitting quota requests across multiple Azure subscriptions
     5→# Requirements: jq, Azure CLI (az)
     6→
     7→set -euo pipefail
     8→umask 077
     9→
    10→# Configuration
    11→# CLI argument parsing
    12→PROCESS_ALL=false
    13→DRY_RUN=false
    14→OUTPUT_FORMAT="text"
    15→CONFIG_FILE="quota-config.json"
    16→
    17→while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    18→    case "$1" in
    19→        --all)
    20→            PROCESS_ALL=true
    21→            shift
    22→            ;;
    23→        --dry-run)
    24→            DRY_RUN=true
    25→            shift
    26→            ;;
    27→        --format)
    28→            if [[ -z "${2:-}" ]]; then
    29→                echo "Error: --format requires a value (text, json, table)" >&2
    30→                exit 1
    31→            fi
    32→            case "$2" in
    33→                text|json|table) OUTPUT_FORMAT="$2" ;;
    34→                *) echo "Invalid format: $2. Supported: text, json, table" >&2; exit 1 ;;
    35→            esac
    36→            shift 2
    37→            ;;
    38→        -h|--help)
    39→            echo "Usage: $(basename "$0") [--all] [--dry-run] [--format FMT] [config-file]"
    40→            echo "  --all         Process all discovered subscriptions (skip TUI selector)"
    41→            echo "  --dry-run     Validate configuration and auth without submitting requests"
    42→            echo "  --format FMT  Output format: text (default), json, table"
    43→            echo "  config-file   Path to quota config JSON (default: quota-config.json)"
    44→            echo ""
    45→            echo "Subscriptions are processed in parallel. Log lines may interleave."
    46→            exit 0
    47→            ;;
    48→        -*)
    49→            echo "Unknown option: $1" >&2
    50→            echo "Usage: $(basename "$0") [--all] [--dry-run] [--format FMT] [config-file]" >&2
    51→            exit 1
    52→            ;;
    53→        *)
    54→            CONFIG_FILE="$1"
    55→            shift
    56→            ;;
    57→    esac
    58→done
    59→LOG_DIR="$(pwd)"
    60→if [[ ! -w "$LOG_DIR" ]]; then
    61→    LOG_DIR="${TMPDIR:-/tmp}"
    62→fi
    63→LOG_FILE="${LOG_DIR}/quota-requests-$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"
    64→MAX_RETRIES=3
    65→RETRY_DELAY=5
    66→QUOTA_API_VERSION="2023-02-01"
    67→
    68→# Colors for output
    69→RED='\033[0;31m'
    70→GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    71→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    72→BLUE='\033[0;34m'
    73→NC='\033[0m'
    74→
    75→# Logging function
    76→log() {
    77→    local level=$1
    78→    shift
    79→    local message="$*"
    80→    local timestamp
    81→    timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
    82→    local formatted="${timestamp} [${level}] ${message}"
    83→    echo -e "$formatted" >&2
    84→    echo -e "$formatted" | sed 's/\x1b\[[0-9;]*m//g' >> "$LOG_FILE"
    85→}
    86→
    87→# Error handler
    88→error_exit() {
    89→    log "ERROR" "${RED}$1${NC}"
    90→    exit 1
    91→}
    92→
    93→# Classify API error as transient (retry) or permanent (fail immediately)
    94→# Args: $1 = az rest stdout (may contain JSON error body)
    95→#       $2 = az rest stderr (contains CLI messages like "429 (Too Many Requests)")
    96→# Returns: "transient" or "permanent"
    97→classify_error() {
    98→    local response=$1
    99→    local stderr_output=${2:-""}
   100→
   101→    # Primary: extract HTTP status code from CLI error text
   102→    # Pattern matches: "Response status code is not successful: '429 (Too Many Requests)'"
   103→    local http_code
   104→    http_code=$(echo "$stderr_output" | grep -oE '[0-9]{3} \(' | head -1 | grep -oE '[0-9]{3}' || echo "")
   105→    if [[ -z "$http_code" ]]; then
   106→        http_code=$(echo "$response" | grep -oE '[0-9]{3} \(' | head -1 | grep -oE '[0-9]{3}' || echo "")
   107→    fi
   108→
   109→    if [[ -n "$http_code" ]]; then
   110→        case "$http_code" in
   111→            429|503|504) echo "transient"; return ;;
   112→            400|401|403|404|409) echo "permanent"; return ;;
   113→        esac
   114→    fi
   115→
   116→    # Fallback: check error.code from JSON body (stdout or embedded in stderr)
   117→    local error_code
   118→    error_code=$(echo "$response" | jq -r '.error.code // empty' 2>/dev/null) || error_code=""
   119→    if [[ -z "$error_code" && -n "$stderr_output" ]]; then
   120→        error_code=$(echo "$stderr_output" | grep -o '{[^{}]*}' | head -1 | jq -r '.error.code // empty' 2>/dev/null) || error_code=""
   121→    fi
   122→
   123→    case "$error_code" in
   124→        TooManyRequests|RequestRateLimitExceeded|ServiceUnavailable|GatewayTimeout)
   125→            echo "transient"; return ;;
   126→        BadRequest|InvalidParameter|AuthenticationFailed|InvalidAuthenticationToken|\
   127→AuthorizationFailed|Forbidden|ResourceNotFound|NotFound|Conflict)
   128→            echo "permanent"; return ;;
   129→    esac
   130→
   131→    # Default: no HTTP code extractable (network error, DNS failure, timeout) → retry
   132→    echo "transient"
   133→}
   134→
   135→# Check dependencies
   136→check_dependencies() {
   137→    log "INFO" "${BLUE}Checking dependencies...${NC}"
   138→    
   139→    local deps=("jq" "az")
   140→    for cmd in "${deps[@]}"; do
   141→        if ! command -v "$cmd" &> /dev/null; then
   142→            error_exit "Required command '$cmd' not found. Please install it and try again."
   143→        fi
   144→    done
   145→    
   146→    log "INFO" "${GREEN}All dependencies are available${NC}"
   147→}
   148→
   149→# Validate config file
   150→validate_config() {
   151→    log "INFO" "${BLUE}Validating configuration file...${NC}"
   152→
   153→    if [[ ! -f "$CONFIG_FILE" ]]; then
   154→        error_exit "Configuration file not found: $CONFIG_FILE"
   155→    fi
   156→
   157→    local jq_err
   158→    jq_err=$(jq empty "$CONFIG_FILE" 2>&1) || error_exit "Invalid JSON in configuration file: $jq_err"
   159→
   160→    # Detect config format: multi-model (models array) vs legacy (top-level fields)
   161→    local has_models
   162→    has_models=$(jq -e '.models | type == "array"' "$CONFIG_FILE" 2>/dev/null) && has_models=true || has_models=false
   163→
   164→    # Delegate all per-field validation to validate_config_fields
   165→    validate_config_fields "$has_models"
   166→}
   167→
   168→# Validate all config fields, collecting errors and reporting them at once
   169→# Args: $1 = "true" if multi-model format, "false" for legacy
   170→validate_config_fields() {
   171→    local has_models=$1
   172→    local validation_errors=()
   173→
   174→    # --- Common validations (both formats) ---
   175→
   176→    # Requestor fields (show field NAMES only — no PII values in errors)
   177→    local email
   178→    email=$(jq -r '.email // empty' "$CONFIG_FILE")
   179→    if [[ -z "$email" ]]; then
   180→        validation_errors+=("requestor email is required")
   181→    fi
   182→
   183→    local first_name
   184→    first_name=$(jq -r '.firstName // empty' "$CONFIG_FILE")
   185→    if [[ -z "$first_name" ]]; then
   186→        validation_errors+=("requestor firstName is required")
   187→    fi
   188→
   189→    # Subscription entries (only if subscriptions key exists — legacy configs
   190→    # may omit this entirely and discover subs from az account list at runtime)
   191→    local has_subscriptions
   192→    has_subscriptions=$(jq -e '.subscriptions | type == "array"' "$CONFIG_FILE" 2>/dev/null) && has_subscriptions=true || has_subscriptions=false
   193→
   194→    if [[ "$has_subscriptions" == "true" ]]; then
   195→        local sub_count
   196→        sub_count=$(jq '.subscriptions | length' "$CONFIG_FILE")
   197→
   198→        if [[ "$sub_count" -eq 0 ]]; then
   199→            validation_errors+=("subscriptions array is empty")
   200→        else
   201→            local i=0
   202→            while [[ $i -lt $sub_count ]]; do
   203→                local sub_id
   204→                sub_id=$(jq -r --argjson idx "$i" '.subscriptions[$idx].id // empty' "$CONFIG_FILE")
   205→                if [[ -z "$sub_id" ]]; then
   206→                    validation_errors+=("subscriptions[$i].id is required")
   207→                fi
   208→
